nodes.label: SPM_BG / *_BG ambient-field global API
nodes.rationale: All dashboards expose a tiny global background API (setAccent/setGlass/setCondition) on window so app logic can drive the WebGL field without a hard dependency; the API is defined up-front as no-ops so calls are safe before init or when WebGL is unavailable. COCOM/ECG/formulary use window.SPM_BG (setAccent + setGlass); humanoid reuses the PFC field under window.PFC_BG (setCondition + setGlass).

nodes.label: Standalone single-file inline build pattern
nodes.rationale: Each dashboard ships an offline *_Standalone.html built by build_standalone.py, which reads index.html and string-replaces each <link>/<script src> tag with inline <style>/<script> blocks, escaping </script as <\/script. Humanoid extends the pattern: it also base64-inlines fonts and Spline scene/WASM/JS as data: URLs.

nodes.label: EDITMODE tweak host postMessage protocol
nodes.rationale: useTweaks/TweaksPanel implement a host edit-mode protocol over postMessage: panel posts __edit_mode_available, listens for __activate/__deactivate_edit_mode, and persists edits via __edit_mode_set_keys (host rewrites the /*EDITMODE-BEGIN*/ JSON block on disk). TWEAK_DEFAULTS is the single source of truth consumed by scene-app's App.

nodes.label: Cross-component sharing via window.* registry
nodes.rationale: Components communicate by reading/writing window.* globals (PCC_SCENARIOS, PCC_SOUND, PFC_BG, ECG_LEADS, registered components). This lets the multi-file build and the single-file standalone build (each component IIFE-wrapped) share state identically without a module bundler.

nodes.label: EDITMODE host/postMessage tweak protocol
nodes.rationale: Tweak values live in a /*EDITMODE-BEGIN*/ JSON block; useTweaks posts __edit_mode_set_keys to the host which rewrites the block on disk. App and TweaksPanel sync open/close state through __activate/__deactivate/__dismissed postMessages.

nodes.label: Patient-condition-driven ambient field
nodes.rationale: The WebGL background colour tracks scenario priority (stable/urgent/critical) and heartbeat tempo follows HR, giving an at-a-glance ambient read of patient state; pharm reuses the same engine but drives it by drug tier and prompts by active category.

nodes.label: Scenario Card Structure (OPQRST/Vitals/Dx/Q&A/Actions/Pearls)
nodes.captured_at: Each scenario follows a fixed didactic template: Character Development, Environment, Clinical Presentation, OPQRST, Vital Signs, Physical Examination, hidden Differential Diagnosis, answer-keyed Critical-Thinking Questions, Critical Actions checklist, Clinical Pearls, and Resolution. Reveal/toggle interactions support Socratic self-testing.

nodes.norm_label: rabies pep (wound wash + rig + vaccine series)

nodes.label: VHF Infection-Control / Isolation Posture
nodes.captured_at: Undifferentiated hemorrhagic fevers are treated as potentially transmissible (Ebola/Marburg cannot be excluded clinically) until PCR confirmation; default to standard+contact+droplet precautions while not delaying life-saving evacuation.

nodes.label: WHO Rabies Exposure Categories (I/II/III)
nodes.captured_at: Category III transdermal bite mandates immediate wound washing, full vaccine series, and RIG; rabies once symptomatic is essentially 100% fatal so PEP is the only lever.

nodes.label: Disease and Non-Battle Injury (DNBI) Dominance
nodes.captured_at: In the AFRICOM AOR the dominant medical threat is disease and non-battle injury, not gunfire — driving the tropical/infectious-disease emphasis of the scenario set.

nodes.label: AFRICOM (U.S. Africa Command)
nodes.rationale: All scenarios in this range are AFRICOM AOR cases (Central African rainforest, Ethiopian highlands, Horn of Africa, Somalia) using the 'af-sc' id prefix.

nodes.label: Ivermectin-Induced Encephalopathy (high Loa load)
nodes.rationale: Fatal encephalopathy when ivermectin is given to patients with very high Loa microfilarial loads (>~30,000 mf/mL).

nodes.label: Test-and-Not-Treat Strategy
nodes.rationale: Measure daytime-blood Loa microfilarial load before ivermectin; withhold if load is dangerously high. The core safeguard against iatrogenic encephalopathy.

nodes.label: High-Altitude Cerebral Edema (HACE)
nodes.rationale: AMS progressed to brain swelling; cardinal signs ataxia + altered mentation.

nodes.label: High-Altitude Pulmonary Edema (HAPE)
nodes.rationale: Non-cardiogenic pulmonary edema; leading cause of altitude death.

nodes.label: Descent (definitive treatment for HACE/HAPE)
nodes.rationale: Descent directly reverses the hypoxic driver; drugs and oxygen are bridges, not substitutes.

nodes.label: Wilderness Medical Society (WMS) Guidance
nodes.rationale: Cited for graded-ascent prophylaxis (altitude) and 3% hypertonic saline dosing (EAH).

nodes.label: Doxycycline
nodes.rationale: Recurs across cases: brucellosis combination tx, and empiric leptospirosis/rickettsial coverage.

nodes.label: Avoid Rifampin where TB Co-Endemic
nodes.rationale: Antimicrobial-stewardship choice: prefer doxycycline+aminoglycoside over doxycycline+rifampin to avoid promoting rifampin-resistant TB.

nodes.label: One-Health / Zoonotic Partner-Force Medicine
nodes.rationale: Human-animal-environment interface framing shared by brucellosis and leptospirosis cases.

nodes.label: Empiric Doxycycline Covers Rickettsial Mimic
nodes.rationale: In East Africa, doxycycline covers both leptospirosis and endemic rickettsial disease when undifferentiated; penicillin/cephalosporins do not.

nodes.label: Ceftriaxone
nodes.rationale: Used for severe leptospirosis and, per TCCC Change 25-1, as the open-combat-wound antibiotic replacing ertapenem.

nodes.label: Scenario 24 — Sickle Cell Trait & Exertional Rhabdomyolysis (Operation Iron Selection)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 24 — sickle cell trait & exertional rhabdomyolysis (operation iron selection)

nodes.label: Hyperkalemia Algorithm (calcium / insulin+dextrose / remove)
nodes.rationale: Acute killer in rhabdo and crush syndrome; same recurring austere-care algorithm referenced in Scenario 40.

nodes.label: Nelson et al., NEJM 2016 (Army SCT cohort)
nodes.author: Nelson et al.
nodes.rationale: ~48,000-soldier cohort: SCT not linked to higher death under precautions but linked to higher exertional rhabdomyolysis — the evidence-updated framing.
nodes.norm_label: nelson et al., nejm 2016 (army sct cohort)

nodes.label: Rectal Core Temperature (key discriminator)
nodes.rationale: Single most useful field discriminator separating exertional heat stroke (>40C) from rhabdo and EAH.

nodes.label: Exercise-Associated Hyponatremia (EAH)
nodes.rationale: Dilutional water intoxication; the collapse you must NOT treat with more water — treatment is opposite of dehydration.

nodes.label: Drink-to-Thirst Prevention
nodes.rationale: Counters the 'overdrink to beat dehydration' dogma that causes EAH; the conscientious heavy drinker is the classic victim.

nodes.label: Neurotoxic Elapid Envenomation (Black Mamba / Cobra)
nodes.rationale: Descending paralysis with minimal local injury; mirror image of cytotoxic viper bite.

nodes.label: Pressure Immobilization (indicated for neurotoxic bite)
nodes.rationale: Indicated for neurotoxic elapid bites without local necrosis — opposite of the contraindication for cytotoxic viper bites.

nodes.label: Assisted Ventilation (the lifesaver)
nodes.rationale: A paralyzed but ventilated patient survives; the ventilation marathon links envenomation to prolonged care.

nodes.label: Junctional Hemorrhage
nodes.rationale: Groin/axilla/neck bleed too proximal for a limb tourniquet — leading preventable death in the post-tourniquet era.

nodes.label: Whole Blood / Walking Blood Bank (LTOWB)
nodes.rationale: Low-titer O whole blood is TCCC/JTS resuscitation fluid of choice; ROLO / Valkyrie programs; recurs in Scenarios 29, 33, 36.

nodes.label: Tranexamic Acid (TXA, single 2 g dose)
nodes.rationale: Current TCCC simplified to single 2 g IV/IO; 3-hour window removed (Ukraine data, benefit to 12 h).

nodes.label: MARCH Algorithm (TCCC)
nodes.rationale: Massive hemorrhage first; structural sequence referenced across all combat-trauma scenarios (27-33).

nodes.label: TCCC Change 25-1 (ceftriaxone for open wounds)
nodes.rationale: Doctrinal update: ceftriaxone 2 g replaced ertapenem for open combat wounds; cited in Scenarios 27 and 41.

nodes.label: Scenario 28 — Tension Pneumothorax (Operation Hollow Reed)
nodes.rationale: Two-site needle decompression and finger thoracostomy; MARCH Respiration.

nodes.label: Scenario 29 — Hemorrhagic Shock / Damage-Control Resuscitation (Operation Crimson Ledger)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 29 — hemorrhagic shock / damage-control resuscitation (operation crimson ledger)

nodes.norm_label: scenario 30 — airway trauma & surgical cricothyroidotomy (operation shattered jaw)

nodes.label: Ketamine (analgesia)

nodes.label: Prolonged Casualty Care (PCC / PFC)

nodes.label: Scenario 36 — Prolonged Damage-Control Resuscitation (Operation Crimson Reserve)

nodes.norm_label: scenario 36 — prolonged damage-control resuscitation (operation crimson reserve)

nodes.norm_label: scenario 37 — nursing fundamentals in pcc (operation quiet vigil)

nodes.norm_label: scenario 38 — telemedicine in prolonged field care (operation distant voice)

nodes.label: Scenario 39 — Sepsis in Prolonged Field Care (Operation Slow Burn)

nodes.rationale: Header at lines 2037-2042 (within range); leave war wounds open, copious irrigation, conservative debridement, delayed primary closure.

nodes.label: Scenario Card Structure (sc-head / sc-body)
nodes.rationale: Shared HTML template: each scenario is a <section class=scenario> with header, OPQRST, vitals, differential, Q&A, critical actions, pearls, resolution.

nodes.label: data-search Filter Attribute
nodes.rationale: Long normalized keyword string on each scenario section enabling client-side search/filter.

nodes.label: Scenario 41: Wound Care & Debridement in PCC (Operation Tide)
nodes.rationale: War wounds are heavily contaminated and tetanus-prone; the doctrine is to clean and leave OPEN for delayed primary closure, not field-suture, because closing traps infection over a multi-day hold.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 41: wound care & debridement in pcc (operation tide)

nodes.label: Irrigation & Conservative Debridement
nodes.rationale: 'The solution to pollution is dilution' — copious low-pressure irrigation; field debridement is conservative (obvious debris/dead tissue only); aggressive surgical debridement is a surgeon's job.

nodes.label: Open Wound / Delayed Primary Closure
nodes.rationale: Closing a contaminated war wound in the field creates an anaerobic environment for abscess and gas gangrene; leave open, redress on schedule, close later at a surgical facility.

nodes.label: Invasive / Necrotizing Soft-Tissue Infection
nodes.rationale: Surgical emergency antibiotics alone cannot control; recognized by spreading erythema, crepitus, foul odor, pain out of proportion — triggers broadened antibiotics, teleconsult, urgent evacuation.

nodes.label: Scenario 42: AKI Without Dialysis (Operation Dry River)
nodes.rationale: Dialysis is unavailable in the field, so once kidneys fail you manage consequences by hand; emphasis is prevention/protection — keep kidneys perfused and unburdened.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 42: aki without dialysis (operation dry river)

nodes.label: Acute Kidney Injury (pre-renal + myoglobinuric)
nodes.rationale: Pre-renal injury from hypoperfusion/shock plus intrinsic injury from myoglobin (crush/rhabdo) precipitating in tubules; oliguria, dark urine, hyperkalemia risk.

nodes.label: Hyperkalemia (the acute killer of AKI)
nodes.rationale: Treated with calcium (membrane stabilize) -> insulin+dextrose/albuterol/bicarb (shift) -> binding resins (limited removal without dialysis); ECG shows peaked T waves, wide QRS.

nodes.label: Nephrotoxin Avoidance & Renal Dose Adjustment
nodes.rationale: Pull NSAIDs (meloxicam), aminoglycosides; avoid further hypotension/ischemic hits — 'do no further harm to the kidney' is much of conservative AKI care.

nodes.label: Foley / Hourly Urine-Output Monitoring
nodes.rationale: Key perfusion/renal trend gauge in PCC; central monitor for AKI management.

nodes.label: Scenario 43: Ventilation & Oxygenation in PCC (Operation Steady Breath)
nodes.rationale: Securing an airway is the easy TCCC skill; ventilating correctly for hours with a transport ventilator, finite oxygen, and limited monitoring is the hard sustained PCC critical-care task.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 43: ventilation & oxygenation in pcc (operation steady breath)

nodes.label: Lung-Protective Ventilation
nodes.rationale: Low tidal volumes 6-8 mL/kg ideal body weight, rate to normal capnography-guided CO2, adequate PEEP, lowest FiO2 keeping SpO2 92-96%, limit airway pressures.

nodes.label: Oxygen Stewardship / Conservation
nodes.rationale: Oxygen is heavy, finite, not easily resupplied; titrate to lowest effective FiO2 (target SpO2 92-96, don't chase 100), calculate supply duration, plan resupply.

nodes.label: Capnography (EtCO2 Monitoring)
nodes.rationale: Confirms airway placement, guides ventilation to normal CO2 (critical in TBI), and gives early warning of displacement/obstruction/circulatory collapse before SpO2 does.

nodes.label: DOPE Mnemonic (vent deterioration)
nodes.rationale: Displacement, Obstruction, Pneumothorax, Equipment — disconnect and manually bag while troubleshooting to distinguish patient airway problem from equipment problem.

nodes.label: Scenario 44: Documentation in PCC (Operation Paper Trail)
nodes.rationale: Documentation is a clinical tool, not bureaucracy — what isn't written is lost; it reveals trends, prevents medication errors, supports teleconsult, and powers the handoff.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 44: documentation in pcc (operation paper trail)

nodes.label: TCCC Casualty Card (DD Form 1380)
nodes.rationale: Standardized battlefield record that travels with the patient: injuries, MARCH interventions with times, fluids/blood, meds (drug/dose/route/time), vitals; filled as care is given.

nodes.label: PCC Flow Sheet (trend data)
nodes.rationale: Captures the trend over time (serial vitals, hourly urine output, mentation, meds, wounds, vent settings) — the trend is the master diagnostic in PCC.

nodes.label: MIST Structured Handoff
nodes.rationale: Mechanism, Injuries, Signs/trends, Treatments — expanded for PCC; verbal structured handoff plus written record ensure continuity at evacuation.

nodes.label: Scenario 45: Analgesia, Sedation & Delirium Over Days (Operation Long Night)
nodes.rationale: Sustained titration between suffering and over-sedation over days; new agitation is a vital sign requiring evaluation, not just heavier sedation.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 45: analgesia, sedation & delirium over days (operation long night)

nodes.label: Delirium (hyperactive/hypoactive)
nodes.rationale: Acute fluctuating disturbance of attention/cognition; new agitation can signal reversible killers (hypoxia, hypotension, hypoglycemia, sepsis, uremia) — rule those out first, manage non-drug measures before pharmacology.

nodes.label: Scenario 46: Tourniquet Conversion (Operation Slack Line)
nodes.rationale: A tourniquet that saved a life can cost a limb if left on unnecessarily; conversion is a deliberate criteria-based reassessment balancing ischemia vs re-hemorrhage. When in doubt leave it and evacuate — a life outranks a limb.

nodes.label: Tourniquet Conversion Criteria
nodes.rationale: Convert only if stable/resuscitated, bleeding controllable by packing/pressure, can monitor and reapply, and within ~6h window. Do not convert if unstable, uncontrollable bleed, amputation/non-salvageable, can't monitor, or >6h elapsed.

nodes.label: Hemostatic Gauze / Wound Packing & Pressure Dressing
nodes.rationale: The conversion method: pack wound, apply pressure dressing, then slowly loosen (don't remove) the tourniquet, leaving it loosened but ready to re-tighten.
nodes.norm_label: hemostatic gauze / wound packing & pressure dressing

nodes.label: Scenario 47: Fresh Whole Blood Collection Over Time (Operation Renewing Well)
nodes.rationale: In a long hold transfusion needs outstrip supply; the medic must safely repeatedly collect fresh whole blood from the team, turning the walking blood bank into a sustained program.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 47: fresh whole blood collection over time (operation renewing well)

nodes.label: Walking Blood Bank (ROLO model)
nodes.rationale: Prescreened low-titer group O donors per JTS Whole Blood Transfusion CPG / Ranger O Low Titer (ROLO); a renewable resource only if donors are protected — an over-bled donor is a self-inflicted second casualty.

nodes.label: ABO / Titer Compatibility Safeguards
nodes.rationale: Group O low-titer minimizes ABO risk but still verify/document donor and recipient; a mismatched transfusion causes fatal acute hemolysis. Give calcium with ongoing transfusion (citrate).

nodes.label: Scenario 48: Austere MASCAL Triage (Operation Hard Choices)
nodes.rationale: Mass-casualty triage inverts single-casualty care: greatest good for the greatest number, maximizing total survivors rather than pouring scarce resources into the unsalvageable.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 48: austere mascal triage (operation hard choices)

nodes.label: Two-Pass Triage (categories Immediate/Delayed/Minimal/Expectant)
nodes.rationale: First pass: rapid categorization + seconds-fast life-saving (tourniquet, airway, decompress). Second pass: full treatment in priority order, reassess and re-triage as categories change.

nodes.label: Expectant Category (resource-dependent)
nodes.rationale: Designating non-survivable-given-resources casualties; resource-dependent and not necessarily permanent — provide comfort, never abandon the human, re-triage if resources change.

nodes.label: Tension Pneumothorax Needle Decompression
nodes.rationale: A salvageable immediate life threat fixed in seconds on the first triage pass.

nodes.label: Scenario 49: The Awake, Suffering & Expectant Patient (Operation Quiet Company)
nodes.rationale: Caring well for a casualty who cannot be saved — comfort, dignity, presence — is skilled medicine, not failure; the goal shifts from cure to relieving suffering.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 49: the awake, suffering & expectant patient (operation quiet company)

nodes.label: Palliative / Comfort Care
nodes.rationale: Aggressive symptom relief (pain via opioids/ketamine titrated freely, air hunger via opioids/positioning/O2, anxiety via sedation); intent is symptom relief not hastening death; never abandon the patient.

nodes.label: Moral Injury & Team/Self Support
nodes.rationale: Witnessing expectant care burdens team and medic; debrief, use behavioral health/chaplain resources — the medic who ignores it risks becoming a delayed casualty themselves.

nodes.label: Scenario 50: PCC Capstone — 72-Hour Multi-System Casualty (Operation Long Shadow)
nodes.rationale: Integrative test synthesizing every PCC competency over 72 hours by one medic; reconcile competing priorities (TBI BP vs hemorrhage permissive hypotension; sepsis vs AKI fluids) and drive evacuation.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 50: pcc capstone — 72-hour multi-system casualty (operation long shadow)

nodes.label: MARC²H³-PAWS-L Reassessment Framework
nodes.rationale: Structured repeated framework to reassess all systems then prioritize by immediate lethality, re-ranking dynamically as trends shift.

nodes.label: Teleconsultation
nodes.rationale: Force multiplier extending an ICU's brain to the hide site; works only when fed accurate documented trend data — garbage data yields garbage advice.

nodes.label: Evacuation Decision / Priority
nodes.rationale: Overarching goal; driven by deteriorating trends, resource exhaustion, or needs unmeetable in field (surgery, dialysis, neurosurgery, ICU). Everything in the hold bridges to definitive care.

nodes.label: TBI Management (higher BP target)
nodes.rationale: Significant TBI favors higher BP (SBP 100-110) to perfuse the injured brain, conflicting with the bleed's permissive hypotension; avoid hypoxia/hypotension, hypertonic saline for herniation.

nodes.label: Sepsis Management (source control)
nodes.rationale: Source control + tiered antibiotics + perfusion-targeted resuscitation; 'hypotension is late' — act on early compensated signals (rising HR/RR, mentation) before BP crashes.

nodes.label: CENTCOM Scenario 10: PCC 72-Hour Hold (Operation Long Night)
nodes.rationale: PCC begins where TCCC's hidden 'evac in an hour' assumption ends; medic becomes 'an ICU of one' — penetrating abdominal trauma held 48-72h by a dust storm and denied air corridor.
nodes.norm_label: centcom scenario 10: pcc 72-hour hold (operation long night)

nodes.label: Damage Control Resuscitation / Permissive Hypotension
nodes.rationale: Titrate to perfusion endpoints (mentation, radial pulse, urine output) not a fixed number; accept permissive lower BP when no TBI to avoid popping clots and exhausting supplies.

nodes.label: Minimum / Better / Best Tiering & Ruck-Truck-House-Plane
nodes.rationale: Converts an impossible 'do everything a hospital would' standard into an achievable resource-honest plan; keeps the medic from freezing at the gap between ideal and available.

nodes.label: Nursing Fundamentals (PCC)
nodes.rationale: Airway tending, hydration/nutrition, urine output, wound care, repositioning, hygiene, eye/mouth care, pain/sedation — fundamentals prevent more deaths than any single procedure over a long hold.

nodes.label: CENTCOM Scenario 11: MERS-CoV Pneumonia After Camel Contact (Operation Dromedary)
nodes.rationale: Severe pneumonia + Arabian Peninsula camel contact = MERS until excluded; identify-isolate-inform first because the largest outbreaks were nosocomial, seeded by unrecognized cases.
nodes.norm_label: centcom scenario 11: mers-cov pneumonia after camel contact (operation dromedary)

nodes.label: Identify-Isolate-Inform (high-consequence pathogen)
nodes.rationale: Mask the patient, mask yourself, separate, notify higher — containment cannot wait for diagnosis; treat the hazard as live the moment it is suspected.

nodes.label: toggleSc() — scenario expand/collapse
nodes.rationale: onclick handler on each sc-head that toggles the scenario body open/closed.

nodes.label: revealDiff() — reveal differential diagnosis
nodes.rationale: onclick handler on the reveal-btn that exposes the hidden differential-diagnosis table after the learner forms their own.

nodes.label: toggleAns() — toggle answer key
nodes.rationale: onclick handler on each q-toggle that reveals the critical-thinking answer key for a question.

nodes.label: Scenario <section> Card Structure
nodes.rationale: Each scenario is a <section class='scenario'> with data-tags and a data-search blob (for filtering/search), a collapsible header, and a body with consistent blocks: Character Development, Environment, Clinical Presentation, OPQRST, Vital Signs, Physical Exam, Differential, Critical-Thinking Questions, Critical Actions, Clinical Pearls, Resolution.

nodes.label: data-search Searchable Text Blob
nodes.rationale: Each scenario section carries a long normalized data-search attribute concatenating the scenario's full text for client-side keyword filtering.

nodes.label: Scenario 11 — MERS-CoV: Severe Pneumonia After Camel Contact (Operation Dromedary)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 11 — mers-cov: severe pneumonia after camel contact (operation dromedary)

nodes.label: Supportive Care for Untreatable Respiratory Virus
nodes.rationale: No licensed antiviral/vaccine for MERS; survival is won through oxygen, lung-protective ARDS management, and organ support while the immune system fights.
nodes.norm_label: supportive care for untreatable respiratory virus

nodes.label: Scenario 12 — White Phosphorus Burns: The Fire That Reignites in Air (Operation Ember)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 12 — white phosphorus burns: the fire that reignites in air (operation ember)

nodes.label: White Phosphorus Burn (chemical + thermal + systemic toxin)
nodes.norm_label: white phosphorus burn (chemical + thermal + systemic toxin)

nodes.label: Keep Wound Continuously Wet (Combustion Control)
nodes.rationale: WP reignites on air exposure when a dressing dries; keeping wounds wet is fire suppression, not comfort care — the central treatment lever is excluding oxygen with water/saline.

nodes.label: Copper Sulfate Contraindicated
nodes.rationale: Old textbook answer retired: absorbed copper causes hemolysis and renal injury with no proven benefit; use a Wood's UV lamp for particle visualization instead. Doctrine evolves with evidence.

nodes.label: WP Systemic Toxicity (hypocalcemia / hyperphosphatemia / arrhythmia)
nodes.rationale: WP kills out of proportion to TBSA via absorbed phosphorus driving lethal electrolyte derangement and cardiac arrhythmia — the burn is the smoke, the electrolyte derangement is the fire.

nodes.label: Scenario 13 — Nerve Agent / Organophosphate Exposure: Antidote Against the Clock (Operation Silent Fang)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 13 — nerve agent / organophosphate exposure: antidote against the clock (operation silent fang)

nodes.label: Atropine (muscarinic blocker)
nodes.rationale: Blocks muscarinic receptors to dry secretions and relieve bronchospasm; titrate to drying of secretions, NOT to heart rate or pupils — under-dosing is a classic fatal error.

nodes.label: Pralidoxime (2-PAM, enzyme reactivator)
nodes.rationale: Reactivates inhibited acetylcholinesterase but only before 'aging' makes the bond irreversible (soman ages fast) — a race against a hardening lock, so give early.

nodes.label: Pyridostigmine Pretreatment
nodes.rationale: Reversibly shields a fraction of enzyme before exposure (e.g., soman) so protected enzyme can recover; it is pretreatment only and does nothing once poisoned — never therapy.

nodes.label: Protect, Decontaminate, Then Antidote
nodes.rationale: A contaminated casualty is a contaminated source; a downed medic treats no one. Decon is both treatment (stop the dose) and force protection (stop secondary contamination of aid station/aircraft).

nodes.label: Scenario 14 — Carpet Viper (Echis) Envenomation: When the Blood Forgets How to Clot (Operation Sawtooth)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 14 — carpet viper (echis) envenomation: when the blood forgets how to clot (operation sawtooth)

nodes.label: 20-Minute Whole Blood Clotting Test (20-min WBCT)
nodes.rationale: Austere coagulation lab: a tube and a clock confirm incoagulable blood (VICC), decide on antivenom, and serially detect recurrence — low-tech, high-yield.

nodes.label: Species/Region-Relevant Antivenom
nodes.rationale: The only definitive treatment; giving plasma/factors/platelets or heparin without antivenom just feeds substrate to still-active venom — neutralize the venom first.

nodes.label: Avoid Snakebite Folklore (no incision/suction/tourniquet/ice)
nodes.rationale: With coagulopathic blood, cutting invites uncontrollable bleeding and a tourniquet worsens necrosis/dumps venom on release; pressure-immobilization is for neurotoxic elapids, not hemotoxic vipers.

nodes.label: Coagulopathy Recurrence Monitoring
nodes.rationale: A normalized clotting test isn't a discharge: venom continues absorbing and antivenom is cleared, so monitor serially and redose.

nodes.label: Scenario 15 — Scorpion Envenomation: The Catecholamine Storm (Operation Fat Tail)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 15 — scorpion envenomation: the catecholamine storm (operation fat tail)

nodes.label: Catecholamine Storm (autonomic short-circuit)
nodes.rationale: Venom alpha-toxins hold sodium channels open, welding the sympathetic accelerator to the floor; treatment logic is to lift the foot off the gas, not neutralize a tissue poison.

nodes.label: Prazosin (alpha-1 blockade)
nodes.rationale: Middle East mainstay for systemic envenomation: counters the catecholamine surge by relaxing vasoconstriction and reducing cardiac strain — treats the consequence, not the venom.

nodes.label: Hypercatecholaminergic Myocarditis / Pulmonary Edema
nodes.rationale: The lethal endpoint; transition from revved-up/hypertensive to pump-failing/hypotensive flips drug choice from offloading (prazosin) to inotropic support (dobutamine).

nodes.label: Shake-Out Gear Discipline (prevention)
nodes.rationale: Scorpions hide in boots/bedding/gear; deny the warm dark spaces through routine inspection rather than relying on luck.

nodes.label: Scenario 16 — Burn-Pit & Particulate Exposure: The Slow Injury You Can't See on a Chest X-ray (Operation Ashfall)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 16 — burn-pit & particulate exposure: the slow injury you can't see on a chest x-ray (operation ashfall)

nodes.label: Small-Airways Disease / Constrictive Bronchiolitis
nodes.rationale: Lives in peripheral airways that standard spirometry and plain films poorly interrogate; near-normal routine tests are a low-resolution sensor that doesn't rule out a real distributed injury.

nodes.label: Exposure History as Diagnostic Backbone
nodes.rationale: The diagnosis is built on the link between airborne-hazard exposure and the respiratory pattern; the history is both the diagnostic anchor and the soldier's path to registry/PACT Act care — skipping it erases both.

nodes.label: VA Airborne Hazards and Open Burn Pit Registry
nodes.norm_label: va airborne hazards and open burn pit registry

nodes.label: Believe the Soldier and the Exposure
nodes.rationale: When a high-performer's exercise tolerance collapses but tests look fine, don't default to deconditioning/anxiety — that's a double clinical and institutional failure; reproduce symptoms with exertion and refer.
nodes.norm_label: believe the soldier and the exposure

nodes.label: Scenario 17 — Severe Falciparum Malaria: Any Fever Is Malaria Until Proven Otherwise (Operation Pale Horse)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 17 — severe falciparum malaria: any fever is malaria until proven otherwise (operation pale horse)

nodes.label: Any Fever in a Malaria Area Is Malaria Until Proven Otherwise
nodes.rationale: A forcing function against complacency: falciparum presents non-specifically and kills fast, so a quick RDT/smear on every febrile soldier (regardless of prophylaxis claims) prevents catastrophe — like treating every weapon as loaded.
nodes.norm_label: any fever in a malaria area is malaria until proven otherwise

nodes.label: IV Artesunate
nodes.rationale: Treatment of choice for severe malaria, replacing quinine; if not immediately available, start interim oral ACT rather than waiting — and watch for delayed post-artesunate hemolytic anemia.

nodes.label: Check Glucose / Correct Hypoglycemia
nodes.rationale: Hypoglycemia is a common, recurrent, reversible complication of severe malaria and a mimic of altered mental status — always check it.

nodes.label: Chemoprophylaxis as Unit Discipline
nodes.rationale: 'Nobody else was getting sick' is survivorship bias — incubation hasn't caught up. Prophylaxis (permethrin, nets, DEET) works only with consistent compliance enforced as a command-supported standard.

nodes.label: Scenario Section Template (OPQRST / Differential / Q&A / Critical Actions / Pearls / Resolution)
nodes.norm_label: scenario section template (opqrst / differential / q&a / critical actions / pearls / resolution)

nodes.label: Prolonged Casualty Care (PCC)
nodes.rationale: Recurring conceptual frame: care that shifts from a point-of-injury sprint to a sustained marathon as evacuation extends into hours/days; outcomes determined by disciplined basics over time.

nodes.label: Scenario 32 — Operation Slow Burn (Sepsis in PCC)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 32 — operation slow burn (sepsis in pcc)

nodes.label: Scenario 33 — Operation Dry Kidney (AKI Without Dialysis)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 33 — operation dry kidney (aki without dialysis)

nodes.label: Scenario 34 — Operation Bellows (Ventilation & Oxygenation in PCC)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 34 — operation bellows (ventilation & oxygenation in pcc)

nodes.label: Scenario 35 — Operation Clean Margin (Wound Care & Infection Prevention)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 35 — operation clean margin (wound care & infection prevention)

nodes.label: Scenario 37 — Operation Red Well (Walking Blood Bank)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 37 — operation red well (walking blood bank)

nodes.label: Scenario 38 — Operation Greater Good (Austere MASCAL Triage)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 38 — operation greater good (austere mascal triage)

nodes.label: Scenario 40 — Operation Paper Trail (Casualty Documentation & Handoff)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 40 — operation paper trail (casualty documentation & handoff)

nodes.label: Scenario 41 — Operation Steady State (Sustained Damage-Control Resuscitation)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 41 — operation steady state (sustained damage-control resuscitation)

nodes.label: Scenario 42 — Operation Cold Iron (Hypothermia & the Lethal Triad)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 42 — operation cold iron (hypothermia & the lethal triad)

nodes.label: Scenario 43 — Operation Second Hit (TBI in Prolonged Care)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 43 — operation second hit (tbi in prolonged care)

nodes.label: Scenario 45 — Operation Slow Fuel (Nutrition & Hydration in PCC)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 45 — operation slow fuel (nutrition & hydration in pcc)

nodes.label: Scenario 46 — Operation Quiet Work (Sustained Nursing Care)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 46 — operation quiet work (sustained nursing care)

nodes.label: Scenario 47 — Operation Overpressure (Primary Blast Injury & Blast Lung)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 47 — operation overpressure (primary blast injury & blast lung)

nodes.label: Scenario 48 — Operation Steady Nerve (Acute Combat & Operational Stress)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 48 — operation steady nerve (acute combat & operational stress)

nodes.label: Scenario 49 — Operation Last Watch (Expectant & Comfort Care)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 49 — operation last watch (expectant & comfort care)

nodes.label: Scenario 50 — Operation Whole Casualty (Multi-System PCC Capstone)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 50 — operation whole casualty (multi-system pcc capstone)

nodes.label: Sepsis / Septic Shock
nodes.rationale: Hypotension is the LAST domino to fall; the body compensates (tachycardia, tachypnea, vasoconstriction) long before BP drops, so recognize by early signs.

nodes.label: Acute Kidney Injury (AKI)
nodes.rationale: Kidneys are a filter you cannot replace forward (no dialysis); multifactorial from myoglobin + hypovolemia + sepsis. Protect via perfusion/flushing.

nodes.label: Hyperkalemia
nodes.rationale: The proximate killer in AKI/crush — failing kidneys can't excrete potassium; calcium stabilizes the heart, insulin/dextrose/bicarb/albuterol shift K+ to buy time to dialysis.

nodes.label: Ventilation-Induced Hypotension
nodes.rationale: Positive-pressure ventilation raises intrathoracic pressure, impeding venous return; sedation compounds the BP drop — anticipate, ensure volume.

nodes.label: Combat Wound Infection / Invasive Fungal Infection (IFI)
nodes.rationale: Dismounted blast + high amputation + heavy soil contamination is the high-risk IFI profile; managed differently with aggressive debridement and Dakin's solution.
nodes.norm_label: combat wound infection / invasive fungal infection (ifi)

nodes.label: 'Died of Wounds' (Delayed Infection Death)
nodes.rationale: A survivable blast can kill days later via infection — the wound is a slow-burning second battle fought across the whole hold.

nodes.label: Hypothermia & the Lethal Triad
nodes.rationale: Cold drives the lethal triad (hypothermia + acidosis + coagulopathy) — the cold that won't let blood clot.

nodes.label: Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI) — Secondary Injury Prevention
nodes.rationale: Guard the brain from the second injury: avoid hypotension (SBP<90) and hypoxia (SpO2<90), which each markedly raise mortality.
nodes.norm_label: traumatic brain injury (tbi) — secondary injury prevention

nodes.label: Pain / Anxiety / Delirium in Sustained Care
nodes.norm_label: pain / anxiety / delirium in sustained care

nodes.label: Primary Blast Lung Injury
nodes.rationale: Shockwave tears the lungs; positive-pressure ventilation must be used with caution due to air-embolism and barotrauma risk.

nodes.label: Source Control (Sepsis)
nodes.rationale: Eliminate the infection source (debride/drain wound, remove infected line) — antibiotics without source control is bailing a boat without plugging the hole.

nodes.label: Take/Give Approach (JTS Sepsis CPG)
nodes.rationale: TAKE = assess (vitals, neuro, wound exam, lactate, urine output); GIVE = treat (antibiotics, fluids, oxygen, telemedicine-guided vasopressors).

nodes.label: Early Appropriate Antibiotics / Prophylaxis
nodes.rationale: Adjunct that buys margin; does not replace mechanical wound care; limit duration to curb resistance (e.g., cefazolin).

nodes.label: Telemedicine-Guided Vasopressors (Norepinephrine First-Line)
nodes.rationale: Started only after fluids and under telemedicine guidance; once on pressors, do NOT keep piling on fluids.

nodes.label: Isotonic Fluid Resuscitation
nodes.rationale: Same fluid, opposite handling decided by kidney response: flush while responsive, restrict once anuric to avoid overload.

nodes.label: Foley Catheter / Urine-Output Monitoring
nodes.rationale: Urine output is the real-time field gauge of renal perfusion and function; resuscitate TO a urine-output target.

nodes.label: Adequate Sedation (Ketamine First-Line)
nodes.rationale: NEVER paralyze without sedation — a paralytic without sedation is catastrophic (aware, paralyzed casualty in silent terror).

nodes.label: PEEP (Positive End-Expiratory Pressure)
nodes.rationale: An invasive airway bypasses the glottis and loses the body's natural PEEP — add it back via BVM valve or vent setting.

nodes.label: Targeted Normoxemia (Avoid Hyperoxia)
nodes.rationale: Hyperoxia can harm and oxygen is a finite austere resource — titrate to adequate saturation, conserve supply.

nodes.label: Wet-to-Dry Dressings / Micro-Debridement
nodes.rationale: Goldilocks balance — moist going in, dried by removal q12-24h; pulls away dead tissue with each change.

nodes.label: Leave Combat Wounds Open (Delayed Closure)
nodes.rationale: Closing traps contamination and breeds lethal infection; exceptions are face, dura, scalp. Don't seal the enemy inside.

nodes.label: Tourniquet Conversion to Dressing
nodes.rationale: A tourniquet trades limb tissue for life; once the life threat passes, buy the limb back. Convert only if all three: not in shock, wound monitorable, not an amputation; within 2h; don't remove if on >6h without monitoring/labs; leave it staged to re-tighten.

nodes.label: Telemedicine / Teleconsultation
nodes.rationale: The PCC Communications pillar made concrete — a co-pilot on the radio; remote critical-care expertise co-manages the casualty.

nodes.label: Casualty Documentation & Handoff (DD1380 / MIST / SBAR)
nodes.rationale: The story that travels with the patient — TCCC card, PFC flowsheet, MIST/SBAR handoff.

nodes.label: Principles-Based Two-Pass MASCAL Triage (NATO T1/T2/T3)
nodes.rationale: Do the most good for the most casualties; includes the expectant category and dynamic re-triage.

nodes.label: Expectant / Comfort Care
nodes.rationale: Dignity when cure is no longer possible — shift from curative to comfort care.

nodes.label: MARC2H3-PAWS-L PCC Framework
nodes.rationale: JTS PCC integration mnemonic used as the capstone framework for the complex multi-system casualty.

nodes.label: Sustained Nursing Care (SHEEP VOMIT)
nodes.rationale: Unglamorous relentless detail — Foley care, pressure-injury prevention — that keeps a casualty alive over the long hold.

nodes.label: Scenario <section> Card Component
nodes.rationale: Repeating collapsible scenario card with sc-head, grid2 blocks, OPQRST/Vitals tables, differential, Q&A, critical actions, pearls, resolution.

nodes.label: References Section (Per-Scenario Citations)
nodes.rationale: Collapsible refs panel; one ref-block per scenario linking external CDC/WHO/JTS/CoTCCC sources.

nodes.label: Scenario 02: Leptospirosis — The Red-Eyed Fever After the River Crossing
nodes.norm_label: scenario 02: leptospirosis — the red-eyed fever after the river crossing

nodes.label: Scenario 03: Severe Dengue — When the Fever Breaks and the Patient Crashes
nodes.norm_label: scenario 03: severe dengue — when the fever breaks and the patient crashes

nodes.label: Scenario 04: Japanese Encephalitis — The Fever That Goes to the Brain
nodes.norm_label: scenario 04: japanese encephalitis — the fever that goes to the brain

nodes.label: Scenario 22: Primary Blast Lung Injury — The Quiet Lung That Fails After the Bang
nodes.norm_label: scenario 22: primary blast lung injury — the quiet lung that fails after the bang

nodes.label: Defervescence / Critical Phase
nodes.rationale: The fever breaking is the danger signal, not the all-clear; vascular permeability peaks at defervescence and plasma leaks just as everyone relaxes — the eye of the storm.

nodes.label: Narrowing Pulse Pressure
nodes.rationale: Signature of compensated dengue shock; watch the gap not the systolic top number — it is the fuel gauge and nearly empty.

nodes.label: JE Enzootic Transmission Cycle (pigs, wading birds, rice paddies)
nodes.rationale: Terrain defines the risk: Culex breed in flooded rice fields and amplify JE between pigs and birds; humans are dead-end hosts. JE is won in planning — vaccinate the exposed force and site bivouacs away from paddies and pigs.
nodes.norm_label: je enzootic transmission cycle (pigs, wading birds, rice paddies)

nodes.label: Submarine-Compartment Analogy (air-filled organs)
nodes.rationale: Air-filled organs (lungs, ears, bowel) are the submarine's compartments; the overpressure wave tears the air-tissue interfaces — the lung is the classic casualty.

nodes.label: Intact Eardrum Does Not Rule Out Blast Lung
nodes.rationale: Tympanic membrane ruptures at lower pressure than the lung is injured; stratify by exposure and serial respiratory assessment, not by hearing.
nodes.norm_label: intact eardrum does not rule out blast lung

nodes.label: Delayed-Onset PBLI Monitoring Window
nodes.rationale: A 'fine' blast casualty is on a delay fuse; PBLI declares over minutes to hours, so monitor on a clock and don't clear early.

nodes.label: Positive-Pressure Ventilation is Double-Edged
nodes.rationale: PPV can worsen arterial gas embolism and pneumothorax in the leak-prone lung; titrate carefully and watch for both.

nodes.label: Scenario 23: Traumatic Amputation — The Tourniquet That Stays On
nodes.rationale: An amputation stump is the explicit exception to tourniquet conversion: mangled vessels won't reliably clot and there is no distal limb to save.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 23: traumatic amputation — the tourniquet that stays on

nodes.label: Amputation Tourniquet Non-Conversion Rule
nodes.rationale: TCCC permits converting a limb tourniquet only if the casualty is not in shock, the wound can be monitored, AND it is not controlling an amputated extremity — amputation fails the last condition by definition.

nodes.label: High-and-Tight CoTCCC Limb Tourniquet
nodes.rationale: If one tourniquet fails, add a second side-by-side proximal to distribute circumferential pressure and occlude high-flow vessels.

nodes.label: Damage-Control Resuscitation (TXA, Calcium, Whole Blood)
nodes.rationale: IV/IO access, TXA within 3 h if criteria met, whole-blood-preferred resuscitation, calcium management, warming.

nodes.label: Prolonged Tourniquet Time / Reperfusion Risk
nodes.rationale: Release at surgery can unleash crush-type physiology (hyperkalemia, myoglobin washout); document time and warn the receiving team.

nodes.label: Scenario 24: Burns — The Rule of Tens and the Airway You Secure Early
nodes.rationale: A burned airway is a sponge that swells shut — intubate while the door is still open.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 24: burns — the rule of tens and the airway you secure early

nodes.label: Inhalation / Facial Burn Airway Injury
nodes.rationale: Singed nasal hair, hoarseness, soot, facial burns signal progressive edema; secure airway early based on trajectory not current comfort.

nodes.label: Rule of Tens (Burn Fluid Resuscitation)
nodes.rationale: JTS adult start point: 10 mL/hr x %TBSA, +100 mL/hr per 10 kg over 80 kg; replaces Parkland as the initial rate; then titrate to urine output.

nodes.label: %TBSA Estimation
nodes.rationale: Exclude superficial burns; field methods are approximations; burns can convert deeper; over/underestimation harm in both directions.

nodes.label: Fluid Creep / Over-Resuscitation
nodes.rationale: Titrate to urine output and reassess at 8-12 h; document every mL on a burn flow sheet.

nodes.label: Circumferential Burn / Escharotomy
nodes.rationale: Inelastic eschar acts as a tourniquet (limb) or corset (chest) as edema builds; surgical incision relieves constriction.

nodes.label: Carbon Monoxide / Cyanide Toxicity
nodes.rationale: SpO2 reads carboxyhemoglobin as oxygenated and lies; treat empirically with high-flow 100% oxygen in enclosed-space fires.

nodes.label: Scenario 25: Crush Syndrome & Compartment Syndrome — The Reperfusion Time Bomb
nodes.rationale: The crushed limb is a dam holding potassium and acid; lifting the slab blows the dam into the heart and kidneys.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 25: crush syndrome & compartment syndrome — the reperfusion time bomb

nodes.label: Reperfusion Injury (Extrication Is the Crisis)
nodes.rationale: Crush medicine inverts intuition: the rescue, not the entrapment, is the dangerous moment; prep fluids and hyperkalemia treatment before lifting.

nodes.label: Rhabdomyolysis
nodes.rationale: Crush, prolonged tourniquet, compartment syndrome, and severe blunt limb trauma all converge on one pathway — same playbook.

nodes.label: Compartment Syndrome
nodes.rationale: Pain out of proportion and pain on passive stretch are earliest signs; pulselessness is late; fasciotomy by qualified personnel for true signs.

nodes.label: Scenario 26: Traumatic Brain Injury — MACE2, Red Flags, and the Mandatory Rest
nodes.rationale: A concussed brain is a jolted vehicle with glitching electronics; rest is the reset and a second hit before recovery risks catastrophe.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 26: traumatic brain injury — mace2, red flags, and the mandatory rest

nodes.label: MACE2 (Military Acute Concussion Evaluation v2)
nodes.rationale: Standardized over gestalt for consistency and a defensible baseline; includes red-flag screen, history, SAC cognitive test, neuro exam, vestibular/ocular screen.

nodes.label: TBI Red-Flag Screen
nodes.rationale: Rule out the killer (declining LOC, repeated vomiting, severe headache, seizures, unequal pupils, focal deficit) before labeling it just a concussion; recheck because a bleed can declare hours later.

nodes.label: Event-Driven Mandatory Concussion Protocol / Return-to-Duty
nodes.rationale: Troops under-report; evaluation is mandatory by exposure (blast within distance, blow). Graded criteria-based RTD; don't let a motivated operator self-clear.

nodes.label: Second-Impact Injury Risk
nodes.rationale: A brain that hasn't reset is vulnerable to far worse breakdown if hit again; rest mandated to prevent this.

nodes.label: Scenario 27: Hemorrhagic Shock & the Walking Blood Bank
nodes.norm_label: scenario 27: hemorrhagic shock & the walking blood bank

nodes.label: Scenario 28: Combat Eye Injury — The Rigid Shield and the Pressure You Never Apply
nodes.rationale: Shield the globe rigidly; never apply pressure to a penetrated eye.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 28: combat eye injury — the rigid shield and the pressure you never apply

nodes.label: Scenario 29: Exertional Heat Stroke — Cool First, Transport Second
nodes.rationale: Cool first, transport second — survival hinges on rapid cooling before evacuation.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 29: exertional heat stroke — cool first, transport second

nodes.label: Scenario 31: Leech Bite (Hirudiniasis) — The Wound That Won't Stop Bleeding
nodes.norm_label: scenario 31: leech bite (hirudiniasis) — the wound that won't stop bleeding

nodes.label: Scenario 32: Asian Snakebite — Neurotoxic vs Hemotoxic
nodes.norm_label: scenario 32: asian snakebite — neurotoxic vs hemotoxic

nodes.label: Scenario 33: Melioidosis — The Soil-Borne Sepsis That Mimics Everything
nodes.norm_label: scenario 33: melioidosis — the soil-borne sepsis that mimics everything

nodes.label: Scenario 34: Murine Typhus — Flea-Borne Fever and the Doxycycline You Don't Delay
nodes.norm_label: scenario 34: murine typhus — flea-borne fever and the doxycycline you don't delay

nodes.label: Scenario 35: Chikungunya — Crippling Joint Pain and the NSAID You Hold Until Dengue Is Out
nodes.norm_label: scenario 35: chikungunya — crippling joint pain and the nsaid you hold until dengue is out

nodes.label: Scenario 36: Field Anaphylaxis — Epinephrine to the Thigh, Now
nodes.norm_label: scenario 36: field anaphylaxis — epinephrine to the thigh, now

nodes.label: Scenario 37: Severe / Cerebral Falciparum Malaria — IV Artesunate
nodes.norm_label: scenario 37: severe / cerebral falciparum malaria — iv artesunate

nodes.label: Scenario 38: Typhoid Fever — Stepwise Fever, Rose Spots, and Drug Resistance
nodes.norm_label: scenario 38: typhoid fever — stepwise fever, rose spots, and drug resistance

nodes.label: Scenario 39: Cholera & Severe Dehydration — Replace the Flood, Fast
nodes.norm_label: scenario 39: cholera & severe dehydration — replace the flood, fast

nodes.label: Scenario 40: Hepatitis A & E — Jaundice from the Water, and the Pregnancy Trap
nodes.norm_label: scenario 40: hepatitis a & e — jaundice from the water, and the pregnancy trap

nodes.label: Scenario 41: Amebic Liver Abscess — Fever, RUQ Pain, and the Anchovy Paste
nodes.norm_label: scenario 41: amebic liver abscess — fever, ruq pain, and the anchovy paste

nodes.label: Scenario 42: Tuberculosis Exposure — The Slow Infection and the Decision to Treat Latency
nodes.norm_label: scenario 42: tuberculosis exposure — the slow infection and the decision to treat latency

nodes.label: Scenario 43: Rabies Exposure — Wash the Wound, Then Win the Race Against a 100% Killer
nodes.norm_label: scenario 43: rabies exposure — wash the wound, then win the race against a 100% killer

nodes.label: Prolonged Field Care (PFC) Theme
nodes.rationale: Recurring tag across trauma scenarios — delayed/denied evacuation forces the medic to sustain definitive-care functions (kidney, monitor, transfusion) for extended holds.

nodes.label: Hold NSAIDs Until Dengue Excluded
nodes.rationale: In chikungunya, NSAIDs are withheld until dengue is ruled out due to bleeding risk.

nodes.label: Wound Washing (15-min soap/water)
nodes.rationale: First and most underrated step; thorough cleansing alone markedly reduces rabies risk and bacterial infection.

nodes.label: WHO Rabies Exposure Categories I/II/III
nodes.rationale: Category III (transdermal bite) requires wound care + vaccine + RIG; category determines whether RIG is needed.

nodes.label: Rabies Immunoglobulin (RIG, passive)
nodes.rationale: Pre-formed antibody infiltrated into/around the wound to bridge the gap until vaccine-induced immunity develops in unvaccinated patients.

nodes.label: Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis (PrEP)
nodes.rationale: Pre-deployment rabies vaccination is high-value force-health-protection for endemic, RIG-scarce environments; pre-vaccinated bite victims need only boosters, no RIG.

nodes.label: Bomb-with-Unseen-Timer Analogy
nodes.rationale: Teaching analogy: rabies incubation is a hidden countdown; you cannot wait-and-see because symptoms mean death, but PEP defuses it during the silent window.

nodes.label: Scenario 44: Soil-Transmitted Helminths
nodes.rationale: Operation Barefoot Ground; chronic low-grade parasitic burden causing iron-deficiency anemia after prolonged barefoot exposure to contaminated soil.

nodes.label: Hookworm
nodes.rationale: Larvae penetrate skin (ground itch); adults feed on intestinal blood causing the classic iron-deficiency anemia.

nodes.label: Strongyloides stercoralis
nodes.rationale: Important exception: not covered by albendazole/mebendazole, needs ivermectin; can autoinfect and hyperinfect in immunosuppression.

nodes.label: Termites-in-the-Floorboards Analogy
nodes.rationale: Teaching analogy: STH are a slow, hidden infestation that quietly degrades readiness rather than a dramatic catastrophe.

nodes.label: Sanitation / Footwear / Hygiene Prevention
nodes.rationale: Footwear blocks skin penetration, hand/food hygiene blocks ingestion, waste disposal blocks soil contamination; cheap and decisive.

nodes.label: Scenario 45: The 72-Hour Hold (PCC)
nodes.rationale: Operation Distant Shore; post-blast casualty stabilized by TCCC then held 72 hours awaiting delayed evacuation.

nodes.label: PCC Nursing Care
nodes.rationale: Airway monitoring, hydration/urine output, pressure-injury prevention, hypothermia prevention, wound/pain care; unglamorous work that decides survival over days.

nodes.label: Anticipation / Proactive Planning
nodes.rationale: Pre-empt predictable deterioration (rebleed, infection, airway loss) by pre-staging equipment and contingency plans during calm stretches.

nodes.label: Team Management / Provider Fatigue
nodes.rationale: A lone exhausted medic is a single point of failure; name a leader, delegate roles, build rest/shift plans, ration logistics.

nodes.label: Goal: Get Out of PCC (drive evacuation)
nodes.rationale: PCC is a bridge not a destination; relentlessly push earliest feasible evacuation since field nursing cannot substitute for a surgeon/ICU.
nodes.norm_label: goal: get out of pcc (drive evacuation)

nodes.label: Sprint-vs-Marathon Analogy
nodes.rationale: TCCC is the sprint (don't die in 10 minutes); PCC is the marathon (stay alive 3 days) requiring different disciplined endurance skills.

nodes.label: Scenario 46: Sepsis in Prolonged Field Care
nodes.rationale: Operation Slow Fire; day-3 wound infection going systemic during a delayed evacuation hold.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 46: sepsis in prolonged field care

nodes.label: Sepsis / Septic Shock
nodes.rationale: Dysregulated systemic response to infection with organ dysfunction; a leading late killer specific to prolonged field care.

nodes.label: JTS Sepsis Management in PFC CPG
nodes.norm_label: jts sepsis management in pfc cpg

nodes.label: Minimum/Better/Best Paradigm
nodes.rationale: Tiered care scaled to the medic's actual resources/skills, matching PFC's variable-resource reality with a non-negotiable floor and stretch goals.

nodes.label: Source Control
nodes.rationale: Co-equal with antibiotics: drain abscess, debride wound, remove infected line; antibiotics alone cannot win if the source keeps feeding the fire.

nodes.label: Fluid Resuscitation / Vasopressors
nodes.rationale: Titrate IV fluids to perfusion markers; vasopressors are a with-telemedicine decision when fluid-refractory.

nodes.label: Fire-Spreading-Through-a-House Analogy
nodes.rationale: Local wound infection (one room) becomes systemic sepsis (whole house); catch it early because an established fire is unwinnable even in an ICU.

nodes.label: Trend Vitals / Avoid 'Stable' Anchoring
nodes.rationale: Serial trending on the flowsheet reveals the subtle slow-burn signature; 'stable' is the most dangerous phrase because it invites you to stop looking.

nodes.label: Scenario 47: Telemedicine-Guided Care
nodes.rationale: Operation Distant Voice; solo medic integrates remote critical-care expertise for decisions beyond routine scope.

nodes.label: Telemedicine / Teleconsultation
nodes.rationale: Core PCC capability bringing specialist depth to the bedside; call early and often, do not let pride/hubris prevent a needed consult.

nodes.label: PACE Communications Plan
nodes.rationale: Primary/Alternate/Contingency/Emergency comms must be planned and tested before deployment because telemedicine fails for comms reasons, not medical ones.

nodes.label: General-Contractor-Calling-Specialists Analogy
nodes.rationale: A SOF medic is a broadly skilled contractor; telemedicine is the phone line to the master specialists, augmenting not replacing the medic's hands and judgment.

nodes.label: toggleAns() JS function
nodes.rationale: Toggles answer-key visibility for Critical-Thinking Question buttons.

nodes.label: revealDiff() JS function
nodes.rationale: Reveals the hidden differential-diagnosis table after the learner forms their own.

nodes.label: toggleSc() JS function
nodes.rationale: Expands/collapses a scenario section via its header onclick.

nodes.label: Critical Actions / Clinical Pearls UI Block
nodes.norm_label: critical actions / clinical pearls ui block

nodes.label: Scenario 14: Mountain Fall Polytrauma — Technical Evacuation (OP STONE FALL)
nodes.rationale: Mountain polytrauma where the medicine and the extraction are one integrated problem on one clock — lifesaving interventions done in place before any vertical move.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 14: mountain fall polytrauma — technical evacuation (op stone fall)

nodes.label: Scenario 15: Crush Injury — Rockfall in a Mountain Pass (OP ROCK SHATTER)
nodes.rationale: Crush syndrome where the crisis arrives at the moment of release; pre-treat with fluids before extrication.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 15: crush injury — rockfall in a mountain pass (op rock shatter)

nodes.label: Scenario 16: Lightning Strike — Alpine Ridgeline (OP IRON SKY)
nodes.rationale: Lightning MASCAL where triage is reversed: resuscitate the apparently dead first because the arrest is a reversible electrical reset.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 16: lightning strike — alpine ridgeline (op iron sky)

nodes.label: Scenario 17: Avalanche Trauma — Hypothermia/Asphyxia/Trauma Triad (OP WHITEOUT TRIAD)
nodes.rationale: Avalanche victims arrive with hypothermia, asphyxia and trauma braided; MARCH prioritizes by speed of killing and warming is part of stopping the bleed.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 17: avalanche trauma — hypothermia/asphyxia/trauma triad (op whiteout triad)

nodes.label: Scenario 18: Acute Knee Injury — The Descent Casualty (OP DESCENT TWIST)
nodes.rationale: Mountain MSK injuries cluster on the fatigued loaded descent; a non-weight-bearing joint is an evacuation and hypothermia problem, not just orthopedic.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 18: acute knee injury — the descent casualty (op descent twist)

nodes.label: Scenario 19: Exertional Rhabdomyolysis — Cold-Weather Ruck (OP HEAVY LOAD)
nodes.rationale: Cola-colored urine after extreme exertion is rhabdomyolysis until proven otherwise; cold blunts thirst and masks muscle pain.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 19: exertional rhabdomyolysis — cold-weather ruck (op heavy load)

nodes.label: Scenario 20: Exertional Hyponatremia — The Over-Hydrated Climber (OP SUMMIT FOG)
nodes.rationale: Exertional hyponatremia is the mirror image of dehydration; mimics AMS/HACE but the fluid plan is opposite — do not push fluids.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 20: exertional hyponatremia — the over-hydrated climber (op summit fog)

nodes.label: Scenario 21: MASCAL Triage Under Fire (OP — care under fire / NATO corridor)
nodes.rationale: MASCAL triage maximizes total survivors, not the outcome of any one casualty; care under fire = cover + tourniquets, defer the rest.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 21: mascal triage under fire (op — care under fire / nato corridor)

nodes.label: Scenario 22: FPV Drone Blast & Fragmentation — Multi-Region Penetrating Trauma (OP VIPER DRONE)
nodes.rationale: FPV drones make multifocal frag plus junctional bleeding a routine pattern; treat the killer bleed, replace blood with blood, treat under concealment.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 22: fpv drone blast & fragmentation — multi-region penetrating trauma (op viper drone)

nodes.label: Scenario 23: Primary Blast Lung Injury — Enclosed-Space Explosion (OP SHATTERED WALL)
nodes.rationale: Primary blast lung shreds the lung with no entry wound; enclosed space multiplies the dose; deceptive and evolving — observe and evacuate.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 23: primary blast lung injury — enclosed-space explosion (op shattered wall)

nodes.label: Scenario 24: Hemorrhagic Shock + Hypothermia — Trauma Triad in Winter (OP COLD TRIAD)
nodes.rationale: The trauma triad (hypothermia/acidosis/coagulopathy) is a self-feeding death spiral; warming is hemorrhage control.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 24: hemorrhagic shock + hypothermia — trauma triad in winter (op cold triad)

nodes.label: MARCH Framework (Massive hemorrhage, Airway, Respiration, Circulation, Hypothermia)
nodes.rationale: The tie-breaker that sequences interventions by how fast each problem kills; forces treating the killer bleed over visible minor wounds and reshuffles as you intervene.
nodes.norm_label: march framework (massive hemorrhage, airway, respiration, circulation, hypothermia)

nodes.label: OPQRST Assessment
nodes.rationale: Structured symptom history (Onset, Provocation, Quality, Region, Severity, Time) used in each scenario.

nodes.label: Trauma Triad of Death (hypothermia/acidosis/coagulopathy)
nodes.rationale: Self-feeding death spiral where bleeding causes shock/acidosis and heat loss, and cold plus acid cripple clotting; breaking any one corner, especially warming, helps break the cycle.

nodes.label: Traction Splint (hemostatic device)
nodes.rationale: A closed femur fracture banks 1-1.5L of blood; the traction splint realigns the bone and tamponades bleeding — a hemostatic device, not just comfort.

nodes.label: Spinal Precautions / Vacuum Mattress Litter
nodes.rationale: Perfect spinal immobilization is sometimes incompatible with a complex vertical rescue — a deliberate, documented risk decision.

nodes.label: Technical Rope-Rescue Evacuation
nodes.rationale: Medicine and ropework are one integrated operation with one commander, one timeline and a weather/daylight turn-time.

nodes.label: Crush Syndrome (Reperfusion Injury)
nodes.rationale: Trapped muscle is a chemical pressure cooker; lifting the rock floods central circulation with potassium, acid and myoglobin — the rescue triggers the crisis.

nodes.label: Myoglobinuria / Acute Kidney Injury
nodes.rationale: Myoglobin is renal sludge that precipitates in acidic low-volume kidneys; dark tea-colored urine is the warning.

nodes.label: Aggressive IV Crystalloid / Fluid Resuscitation
nodes.rationale: Fluids are the river that dilutes the poison and flushes the kidneys; front-load before extrication when possible.

nodes.label: Calcium / Albuterol / Sodium Bicarbonate (hyperkalemia field countermeasures)
nodes.rationale: Calcium stabilizes the cardiac membrane; albuterol and bicarbonate shift potassium into cells.
nodes.norm_label: calcium / albuterol / sodium bicarbonate (hyperkalemia field countermeasures)

nodes.label: Tourniquet Use
nodes.rationale: In crush, reserve the TQ for hemorrhage or non-viable limbs, not prophylactic toxin control — a deliberate documented decision; limb tourniquets fail at junctions.

nodes.label: Lightning-Induced Cardiac/Respiratory Arrest
nodes.rationale: The strike acts like a massive defibrillator stunning the heart and breathing center — often a reversible reset with prompt CPR and sustained ventilation.

nodes.label: Reverse Triage (lightning)
nodes.rationale: Lightning flips the normal rule: resuscitate the apparently dead first because their arrest is the one a few minutes of CPR can bring back, and lightning rarely produces a second arrest in the others.

nodes.label: Keraunoparalysis
nodes.rationale: Temporary lightning paralysis from vascular spasm and nerve stunning that mimics spinal/vascular injury but typically resolves over hours.

nodes.label: Aggressive Sustained Ventilation
nodes.rationale: Prolonged apnea after a heartbeat returns drives a secondary cardiac arrest — keep breathing for them until their own drive returns.

nodes.label: Rescuer / Scene Safety (active storm, drone threat)
nodes.rationale: You cannot help anyone as the next casualty; scene safety is a live ongoing decision — move off exposed terrain in a storm, move to concealment under drones.
nodes.norm_label: rescuer / scene safety (active storm, drone threat)

nodes.label: Hypothermia Prevention / Aggressive Rewarming
nodes.rationale: In winter trauma warming is a hemorrhage-control intervention because clotting enzymes fail in the cold; warm aggressively but handle gently to protect the cold heart.

nodes.label: Open Tib/Fib Fracture with Active Bleeding
nodes.norm_label: open tib/fib fracture with active bleeding

nodes.label: Ottawa Knee/Ankle Rules
nodes.rationale: Decision logic for whether to treat as fracture based on tenderness and inability to bear weight.

nodes.label: Neurovascular Status Check (pulses/sensation/motor)
nodes.rationale: Neurovascular status plus weight-bearing ability set the evacuation tier.

nodes.label: Rigid Splinting in Position of Function
nodes.rationale: Splint to prevent the joint buckling on the next step.
nodes.norm_label: rigid splinting in position of function

nodes.label: Evacuation Tier Decision (self-evac vs assisted/litter)
nodes.rationale: The exam (stability, weight-bearing, neurovascular status) decides the evacuation tier; warmth and timely descent beat perfect joint protection.

nodes.label: Lab-Gated Return-to-Duty / Training Programming
nodes.rationale: Rhabdo is often a training-design failure not a toughness test; return-to-duty is gated on normalized labs and kidney function with graded loading, recovery days, enforced hydration and risk-factor screening.

nodes.label: Exertional Dilutional Hyponatremia
nodes.rationale: Drowning the blood in plain water; the mirror image of dehydration — too much plain water dilutes blood sodium and swells brain cells.

nodes.label: Acute Mountain Sickness / HACE
nodes.rationale: The dangerous mimic at altitude: shares headache/nausea/altered mentation with hyponatremia but the fluid plan is opposite.

nodes.label: Drinking History as Field Discriminator
nodes.rationale: Heavy plain-water intake plus clear copious urine and edema favors over-hydration; the drinking history is the best field discriminator between hyponatremia and AMS/dehydration.

nodes.label: Electrolyte Hydration Doctrine (drink to thirst, not maximally)
nodes.rationale: Hydration is a Goldilocks problem; pair fluids with sodium and food, both too little and too much water can kill; do not push fluids or over-correct sodium (osmotic demyelination).
nodes.norm_label: electrolyte hydration doctrine (drink to thirst, not maximally)

nodes.label: MASCAL Triage (maximize total survivors)
nodes.rationale: Utilitarian triage under scarcity that maximizes total survivors, not the outcome of any one casualty; comfort the expectant and re-triage as resources change.

nodes.label: Care Under Fire
nodes.rationale: Gain fire superiority/cover and control massive hemorrhage with tourniquets only; defer the rest to tactical field care.

nodes.label: Walking Wounded as Force Multiplier
nodes.rationale: Direct walking wounded into self-aid, buddy-aid, litter carry and security rather than doing it all yourself.

nodes.label: FPV Drone Threat / Casualty Profile
nodes.rationale: A precision frag grenade that hunts and erases the safe rear; medics must expect multifocal frag plus junctional bleeding as routine and treat under concealment from overhead observation.

nodes.label: Whole Blood / Component Therapy + TXA
nodes.rationale: Replace blood with blood — whole blood is the resuscitation fluid of choice carrying oxygen and clotting factors crystalloid cannot; give TXA early within 3 hours.

nodes.label: Permissive Hypotension
nodes.rationale: Resuscitate to a palpable radial pulse/mentation rather than normal BP so you don't pop fragile early clots — especially in a cold coagulopathic patient.

nodes.label: Primary Blast Lung Injury (Barotrauma)
nodes.rationale: The overpressure wave tears the air-blood interface with no entry wound; the injury that gets missed.

nodes.label: Four Mechanisms of Blast Injury (primary/secondary/tertiary/quaternary)
nodes.rationale: Primary = pressure wave shredding air-filled organs; secondary = fragments; tertiary = body thrown; quaternary = burns/toxic/crush.

nodes.label: Enclosed-Space Blast Amplification
nodes.rationale: Reflected overpressure off walls/floor/ceiling can multiply the effective dose several-fold — a major red flag for primary blast lung.

nodes.label: Gentle Ventilation Caveat (avoid aggressive positive pressure)
nodes.rationale: Forcing air into a damaged air-blood barrier risks air embolism and barotrauma; ventilate gently with lowest effective pressures.
nodes.norm_label: gentle ventilation caveat (avoid aggressive positive pressure)

nodes.label: Blast Bowel & Tympanic Membrane Rupture
nodes.rationale: Other air-filled organs the blast wave damages; ruptured eardrums flag a serious blast dose and blast bowel can perforate hours later.

nodes.label: Non-Compressible (Truncal/Junctional) Hemorrhage
nodes.rationale: Not fixable by external pressure alone; the definitive treatment is surgery forward — support clotting and run for the OR.

nodes.label: Scenario 36: Radiological Dispersal / Dirty Bomb (Operation Scattered Dust)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 36: radiological dispersal / dirty bomb (operation scattered dust)

nodes.label: Scenario 37: Waterborne GI Illness (Operation Still Water)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 37: waterborne gi illness (operation still water)

nodes.label: Scenario 38: Whole Blood Transfusion (Operation Frozen Vein)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 38: whole blood transfusion (operation frozen vein)

nodes.label: Scenario 39: Cold-Water Immersion & Near-Drowning (Operation Cold Harbor)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 39: cold-water immersion & near-drowning (operation cold harbor)

nodes.label: Scenario 41: MEDEVAC 9-Line Under Degraded Comms (Operation Iron Bridge)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 41: medevac 9-line under degraded comms (operation iron bridge)

nodes.label: Tick-Borne Encephalitis (TBE)
nodes.rationale: European tick-borne virus North American medics rarely see; biphasic course is a diagnostic trap; no antiviral cure so prevention (vaccine, tick avoidance) is the medic's main weapon.

nodes.label: Biphasic Course (viremic → interval → neuroinvasive)
nodes.rationale: The symptom-free interval disconnects the later neuro illness from the earlier flu and tick exposure; recognizing the biphasic pattern links them back together.

nodes.label: Bacterial Meningitis
nodes.rationale: Shares field picture (fever, neck stiffness, confusion) with TBE; rapidly-fatal treatable emergency that must be urgently excluded.

nodes.label: TBE Vaccine
nodes.rationale: Effective vaccine exists for TBE but no cure once contracted; push vaccination for endemic-area deployers.

nodes.label: Tick Avoidance (permethrin, DEET, tick checks)
nodes.rationale: Layered defense against the bite; daily full-body tick checks; TBE can transmit within minutes so checks help more for Lyme — which is why TBE vaccination matters.

nodes.label: Lyme Disease (Borrelia)
nodes.rationale: The treatable tick disease — catch the bullseye rash early and antibiotics cure it; miss it and it disseminates to joints, heart, and nerves.

nodes.label: Erythema Migrans (bullseye rash)
nodes.rationale: Near-diagnostic; treat on clinical grounds without waiting for serology (often early-negative).

nodes.label: Radiological Dispersal Device (Dirty Bomb)
nodes.rationale: Lethality comes overwhelmingly from blast/frag, not radiation; treat trauma first — never delay MARCH for radiation decon.

nodes.label: External Contamination
nodes.rationale: Radioactive dust on the body — removable by decon; removing clothing eliminates the large majority.

nodes.label: Internal Contamination
nodes.rationale: Particles taken into the body via inhalation/ingestion/wounds — not removable by surface decon; hospital management.

nodes.label: Irradiation / Exposure
nodes.rationale: Being hit by radiation does not make the casualty radioactive — not a hazard, not contagious to handlers.

nodes.label: Decontamination (strip-and-rinse, cleanest-to-dirtiest)
nodes.rationale: Simple and highly effective for external contamination; performed methodically after trauma is stabilized; cover wounds during washing.

nodes.label: Acute Radiation Syndrome (ARS)
nodes.rationale: Dose-dependent and delayed; uncommon in dirty bombs; a triage input assessed at higher care, not an acute resuscitation issue.

nodes.label: Waterborne Gastroenteritis
nodes.rationale: A common-source GI outbreak is a force health protection event; dysentery has historically taken more soldiers off the line than bullets.

nodes.label: Oral Rehydration Solution (ORS)
nodes.rationale: First-line; exploits the sodium-glucose co-transporter to pull salt and water across the inflamed gut wall — escalate to IV when ORS can't keep pace.

nodes.label: Sodium-Glucose Co-Transport
nodes.rationale: Gut physiology that lets ORS work even during diarrheal illness; plain water lacks the salt/glucose to drive absorption.

nodes.label: Field Sanitation / Water Discipline
nodes.rationale: Outbreak control: condemn the contaminated source, enforce water treatment (boil/chemical/filter), hand hygiene and latrine discipline to break transmission.

nodes.label: Force Health Protection
nodes.rationale: Reframes a minor stomach bug as a mission-threatening event — treat the individual and the unit at once.

nodes.label: Whole Blood (resuscitation fluid of choice)
nodes.rationale: Replaces red cells, clotting factors, and volume at once in physiologic balance; minimize crystalloid which dilutes and worsens coagulopathy.

nodes.label: Transfusion Safety Checklist
nodes.rationale: Non-negotiable even under fire — donor eligibility, positive donor/recipient ID, compatibility steps, documentation; a transfusion error can kill as fast as the hemorrhage.

nodes.label: Trauma Triad (hypothermia, acidosis, coagulopathy)
nodes.rationale: Cold transfusion deepens the death spiral — warm the blood and the patient.

nodes.label: NATO Interoperability
nodes.rationale: Allied elements use different blood-product systems, documentation, standards; interoperability planning done in advance lets a medic safely use allied blood and hand off across the seam.

nodes.label: Cold-Water Immersion
nodes.rationale: Kills in stages: cold shock (gasp/aspiration, fastest killer), cold incapacitation (swim failure), hypothermia, post-rescue collapse.

nodes.label: Near-Drowning / Aspiration
nodes.rationale: Fundamentally a hypoxia problem — airway/oxygenation first; treacherous tail of delayed pulmonary deterioration hours later.

nodes.label: Severe Hypothermia
nodes.rationale: A deeply cold heart is electrically irritable — handle gently and keep horizontal; rough handling can trigger VF arrest.

nodes.label: Core Rewarming (afterdrop avoidance)
nodes.rationale: Strip wet clothing, insulate with vapor barrier, apply heat to core/torso not limbs to avoid driving cold peripheral blood to the core (afterdrop).

nodes.label: "Not Dead Until Warm and Dead"
nodes.rationale: Severe hypothermia mimics death but cold may protect the brain; continue/prolong resuscitation and evacuate to active (incl. extracorporeal) rewarming before pronouncing death.

nodes.label: Pre-Release Aggressive Fluid Loading
nodes.rationale: The cornerstone — dilutes the K+/acid surge and protects kidneys via urine flow; freeing a non-fluid-loaded casualty is how the textbook 'rescue death' happens.

nodes.label: Myoglobinuric Acute Kidney Injury
nodes.rationale: Myoglobin precipitates in dehydrated acidic tubules; aggressive fluids to maintain urine flow are central.

nodes.label: Prophylactic Tourniquet Controversy (crush)
nodes.rationale: Routine 'toxin-dam' tourniquet is NOT recommended — sacrifices a salvageable limb and toxins accumulate regardless; reserve for hemorrhage/non-salvageable limb.

nodes.label: 9-Line MEDEVAC Request
nodes.rationale: Standardized shared (incl. NATO) evacuation language — complete, fast, unambiguous; ensures nothing vital is forgotten and the asset knows what it's flying into.

nodes.label: Evacuation Precedence (Urgent/Priority/Routine)
nodes.rationale: Triage of the evacuation pipeline — scarce contested lift goes first to life/limb/eyesight casualties; mis-categorizing wastes irreplaceable assets and kills.

nodes.label: Electronic Warfare (GPS/comms jamming)
nodes.rationale: Attacks the request itself — fall back to map/terrain navigation, pre-briefed pickup points, alternate frequencies, retrans, data burst, runners.

nodes.label: PACE Planning (Primary/Alternate/Contingency/Emergency)
nodes.rationale: Build redundancy for comms and evacuation means; air may not come in contested airspace — ready ground casevac and casualty collection points.

nodes.label: Casualty Documentation (casualty card)
nodes.rationale: The casualty's memory through handoffs — injuries, tourniquet times, meds, fluids, blood, antidotes — must travel with each casualty across the allied seam.

nodes.label: Ataxia (truncal/gait)
nodes.rationale: The hallmark earliest reliable HACE sign; tested in the field by tandem (heel-to-toe) gait — objective, reproducible discriminator from generic fatigue.

nodes.label: Tandem Gait Test
nodes.rationale: Field-expedient sobriety-test-style check for ataxia; a flat patch of ground substitutes for neuro-imaging the medic lacks.

nodes.label: Immediate Descent
nodes.rationale: Lifesaving definitive treatment — restores oxygen, reverses hypoxic edema; assist/carry the ataxic/obtunded patient and minimize exertion.

nodes.label: AMS-HACE Spectrum
nodes.rationale: AMS is the milder form preceding HACE; worsening AMS is the warning track. Stop ascending with AMS, descend if worsening, never ascend with ongoing symptoms.

nodes.label: Portable Hyperbaric Chamber
nodes.rationale: Simulates descent as a bridge when descent is impossible/delayed, alongside oxygen and dexamethasone.

nodes.label: MARCH (TCCC algorithm)
nodes.rationale: Life-saving trauma care (hemorrhage, airway, breathing) is never delayed for radiation decon.

nodes.label: Portable Hyperbaric Chamber
nodes.rationale: Simulates descent; used as a bridge only when descent is impossible or delayed.

nodes.label: Gradual Ascent / Acclimatization (climb high, sleep low)
nodes.norm_label: gradual ascent / acclimatization (climb high, sleep low)

nodes.label: Mucocutaneous Leishmaniasis (Espundia)
nodes.rationale: A land mine that detonates late: a healed cutaneous ulcer can erode the naso-oropharyngeal mucosa months-to-years later.

nodes.label: Cutaneous Leishmaniasis (primary ulcer)
nodes.rationale: Must be treated systemically (not just locally) for mucosa-capable species to prevent later espundia.

nodes.label: Leishmania (V.) braziliensis
nodes.rationale: New World L. Viannia subgenus species with highest mucosal-progression risk in the Andean Amazon.

nodes.label: Sandfly (vector)
nodes.rationale: Crepuscular/nocturnal biter small enough to pass through standard nets; requires fine-mesh/treated nets.

nodes.label: Miltefosine
nodes.rationale: Oral agent with lower cure rates / reported failures against L. braziliensis in some regions — drives species/region identification.

nodes.label: Vector Discipline (permethrin, repellent, nets)
nodes.rationale: Same bite-prevention discipline reduces leishmaniasis, malaria, dengue, Chagas, and arboviruses simultaneously.

nodes.label: Yellow Fever
