nodes.rationale: A fire that seems to die then flares back worse: biphasic illness with a deceptive remission before a ~50%-mortality toxic phase; no antiviral.

nodes.label: Yellow Fever Toxic Phase
nodes.rationale: ~15% of cases; jaundice, hepatic/renal failure, coagulopathy/DIC and hemorrhage with ~50% mortality.

nodes.label: Faget Sign (relative bradycardia with fever)
nodes.rationale: Pulse-temperature dissociation; a valuable bedside pointer toward yellow fever (and typhoid) amid the broad tropical febrile differential.

nodes.label: Yellow Fever Vaccine
nodes.rationale: Safe, highly effective, ~lifelong live-attenuated vaccine; the real answer since there is no cure — pre-deployment requirement for the AOR.

nodes.label: Aggressive Supportive / ICU Care
nodes.rationale: Only available treatment for the toxic phase: organ support, blood products/FFP, dialysis — fought with care the field can't provide.

nodes.label: NSAID/Aspirin Avoidance
nodes.rationale: Avoided in hemorrhagic-fever bleeding diathesis; use paracetamol/acetaminophen instead.

nodes.label: Malaria Exclusion (smear/RDT)
nodes.rationale: Always exclude co-lethal, treatable falciparum malaria in any febrile jaundiced patient — never tunnel on yellow fever.

nodes.label: Mosquito Isolation of Patient
nodes.rationale: Viremic patient is infectious to mosquitoes for ~5 days after onset; net the patient to prevent onward transmission.

nodes.label: Patient SGT Tyler 'Banks' Morrow (yellow fever scenario)
nodes.norm_label: patient sgt tyler 'banks' morrow (yellow fever scenario)

nodes.label: SFC Carla 'Doc' Bautista (18D, yellow fever medic)
nodes.norm_label: sfc carla 'doc' bautista (18d, yellow fever medic)

nodes.label: 'Firestorm' Analogy (rapid lethal malaria)
nodes.rationale: Vivax is a slow-burning ember; falciparum is a firestorm that erupts into multi-organ failure within hours. Reorients the medic from 'a febrile patient to work up' to 'a dying patient to treat NOW'.

nodes.label: 'Flood-Water Fever' Framing
nodes.rationale: The exposure (freshwater/flood/mud contaminated with animal/rodent urine entering via skin breaks/mucous membranes) is the key that unlocks the diagnosis. Calf myalgia + conjunctival suffusion after water exposure should immediately raise leptospirosis and prompt early empiric treatment.

nodes.label: Early Empiric Antibiotics on Suspicion
nodes.rationale: Diagnostics (serology/culture/PCR) are slow or unavailable in the field and disease progresses over days; treat now if suspicion is high, don't let diagnostic uncertainty delay antibiotics.

nodes.label: Riverine Force-Health Protection
nodes.rationale: Riverine ops inherently involve freshwater/flood exposure, a predictable occupational threat: protect skin breaks before water entry, consider doxycycline prophylaxis, educate team to report febrile illness after water exposure.

nodes.label: Scenario 27: Oroya Fever / Bartonellosis (Operation Silent Drain)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 27: oroya fever / bartonellosis (operation silent drain)

nodes.label: NORTHCOM Medical Scenarios Theater
nodes.rationale: USNORTHCOM SOF medical training panel: homeland defense and DSCA across CONUS, Alaska, Canada, Mexico; 50 scenarios, 2025 edition integrating Ranger Medic Handbook protocols. Defining challenge is the medic as integrator into a civilian-led system.

nodes.label: Defense Support of Civil Authorities (DSCA)
nodes.rationale: Domestic operations under civilian authority; Posse Comitatus, DSCA rules, and ICS shape what the medic does. Military supports a civilian-led response.

nodes.label: Incident Command System (ICS) Integration
nodes.rationale: In a DSCA event the medic plugs into the civilian-led ICS: report into incident command, coordinate with medical branch/EMS, use plain language and MIST/SALT, avoid freelancing.

nodes.label: 2025 Ranger Medic Handbook (CBRN/MASCAL/Burns/Hypothermia/Toxicology)
nodes.rationale: Primary reference cited throughout: CBRN protocols, Mass Casualty p.59, TCCC p.14-86, Burns p.54-55, Hypothermia p.122-125, Toxicology. Citations are starting points to verify, not a substitute for current doctrine.

nodes.label: CBRN / Mass-Casualty / Disaster Threat Set
nodes.rationale: Primary medical threats: chemical agents (nerve, vesicant, blood), biological threats (anthrax, smallpox, plague, ricin, botulinum), radiological/nuclear (RDD, reactor, IND), MASCAL (active shooter, IED/VBIED, collapse), natural disasters, civilian-military interface.

nodes.label: Scenario 01 — Nerve Agent Exposure (Operation Vigilant Guardian)
nodes.rationale: SGT Elena Rodriguez, 26, acute cholinergic crisis after mislabeled nerve-agent simulant during Vibrant Response exercise. SLUDGEM + killer B's, SpO2 84%, HR 48. Cited RMH CBRN / JTS CBRN Part 2 / MARK-1 / ATNAA.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 01 — nerve agent exposure (operation vigilant guardian)

nodes.label: Nerve Agent / Organophosphate Cholinergic Toxicity
nodes.rationale: Agent blocks acetylcholinesterase ('off-switch'), acetylcholine floods every cholinergic nerve producing SLUDGEM plus killer B's (bronchorrhea, bronchospasm, bradycardia). The casualty drowns in secretions.

nodes.label: SLUDGEM Toxidrome + Killer B's
nodes.rationale: Salivation, Lacrimation, Urination, Defecation, GI distress, Emesis, Miosis, plus killer B's: bronchorrhea, bronchospasm, bradycardia — the cholinergic-crisis fingerprint.

nodes.label: Atropine (nerve-agent antidote)
nodes.rationale: Muscarinic blocker; dries killing secretions and opens airway. 2 mg IM q5-10 min titrated to DRYING OF SECRETIONS/lungs (not heart rate or pupils); severe cases need 10-20+ mg. Works regardless of aging.

nodes.label: Pralidoxime (2-PAM) — enzyme reactivator
nodes.rationale: Breaks the agent-acetylcholinesterase bond, reactivating the enzyme (helps muscle weakness). 600 mg IM early, only effective before 'aging' makes the bond permanent. A race against the clock.

nodes.label: Acetylcholinesterase 'Aging'
nodes.rationale: The agent-enzyme bond chemically ages from loose to a permanent, irreversible lock; once aged, 2-PAM cannot reactivate that enzyme. Aging time varies by agent (minutes to hours) — why 2-PAM is given early.

nodes.label: Diazepam / Midazolam (CANA) — seizure control
nodes.rationale: Benzodiazepine for nerve-agent seizures and brain protection; given prophylactically in severe exposure. 10 mg IM. Third part of the antidote package.

nodes.label: MARK-1 / ATNAA / CANA Antidote Kit
nodes.rationale: MARK-1 (separate atropine + 2-PAM injectors) and ATNAA (both in one autoinjector) deliver antidotes fast under MOPP gloves. Field doctrine: 3 ATNAAs + 1 CANA for any symptomatic casualty beyond isolated miosis.

nodes.label: MOPP / Self-Protection-First Principle
nodes.rationale: Don MOPP-4 / appropriate PPE before approaching — you cannot help if you become a casualty. Decontaminate (remove clothing, blot liquid agent, RSDL or 0.5% hypochlorite) but never delay antidotes for decon.

nodes.label: Scenario 02 — Active-Shooter Mass Casualty (Operation Guardian Shield)
nodes.rationale: 12 penetrating-trauma casualties (4 immediate, 5 delayed, 2 minimal, 1 expectant) after active shooter at a public event; two SOF medics triage and integrate with civilian EMS. RMH TCCC / Mass Casualty p.59 / SALT.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 02 — active-shooter mass casualty (operation guardian shield)

nodes.label: SALT Triage (Sort-Assess-Lifesaving interventions-Treatment/Transport)
nodes.rationale: CDC-sponsored consensus all-hazard method: global SORT by voice command, individual ASSESS with built-in lifesaving interventions, then Treatment/Transport; adds a distinct EXPECTANT (gray) category. Favored over START for domestic incidents.

nodes.label: START Triage
nodes.rationale: Simple Triage and Rapid Treatment: sorts by walking ability, then respirations, perfusion, mental status into four categories. Contrasted with SALT, which integrates LSIs into triage.

nodes.label: Expectant (Gray) Triage Category
nodes.rationale: Utilitarian decision under scarcity: a non-survivable casualty (e.g. devastating head GSW, fixed pupils, agonal) gets comfort, not scarce resources, balancing compassion with triage discipline; re-triage if resources grow.

nodes.label: MIST Handoff (Mechanism/Injuries/Signs/Treatment)
nodes.rationale: Structured handoff to EMS so they continue care; transport is its own triage of scarce ambulance seats — salvageable + time-critical first.

nodes.label: Dynamic Re-Triage Principle
nodes.rationale: Triage categories are a snapshot of a moving situation; a delayed (T2) casualty can deteriorate to immediate (T1). Continuously reassess and reallocate rather than triage-and-forget.

nodes.label: Scenario 03 — Radiological Dispersal Device / Dirty Bomb (Operation Scarlet Dawn)
nodes.rationale: VBIED laced with radioactive material at a transit hub; blast/fragmentation trauma + external contamination + mass worried-well. RMH CBRN / JTS CBRN Part 3 / Radiation Injury.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 03 — radiological dispersal device / dirty bomb (operation scarlet dawn)

nodes.label: Treat-Trauma-First Principle (RDD)
nodes.rationale: A dirty bomb's lethality is the blast/frag, not the radiation; radiation effects are latent. Life-saving MARCH care is never delayed for radiation decontamination.

nodes.label: Hot/Warm/Cold Zone Decontamination
nodes.rationale: Hot (contaminated), warm (decon/transition), cold (clean) zones; move casualties dirty-to-clean. Removing clothing + gentle washing removes the large majority of external contamination; cover wounds during washing.

nodes.label: External Contamination vs Internal Incorporation vs Irradiation
nodes.rationale: External = material on body, decon removes it. Internal/incorporation = inhaled/swallowed/wound, target of decorporation drugs. Irradiation = being hit by radiation; not contagious, no hazard to handlers.
nodes.norm_label: external contamination vs internal incorporation vs irradiation

nodes.label: Decorporation Agents (Prussian Blue, DTPA, KI)
nodes.rationale: Isotope-matched drugs for internal contamination: Prussian blue traps cesium/thallium in gut; Ca/Zn-DTPA chelates transuranics (plutonium, americium); KI blocks radioiodine thyroid uptake. Higher-care/public-health directed, not a field reflex.

nodes.label: Acute Radiation Syndrome (ARS)
nodes.rationale: Dose-dependent and delayed; requires a substantial whole-body dose (uncommon in RDDs). Assessed via dose estimates, time-to-vomiting, later blood counts. A downstream medical problem, not acute resuscitation.

nodes.label: Mass 'Worried-Well' Management
nodes.rationale: A dirty bomb's psychological reach is the point of the weapon; the worried-well vastly outnumber the truly injured. Screening, decon flow, and calm communication separate them so resources reach the genuinely injured.

nodes.label: Scenario 04 — Inhalational Anthrax Index Case (Operation Pale Envelope)
nodes.rationale: Postal worker with flu-like prodrome crashing to fulminant respiratory failure/shock + widened mediastinum days after suspicious-powder/mail incident. RMH CBRN / JTS CBRN Part 4 / Biological Agents.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 04 — inhalational anthrax index case (operation pale envelope)

nodes.label: Inhalational Anthrax (Bacillus anthracis)
nodes.rationale: Biphasic: nonspecific flu-like prodrome (sometimes brief improvement) then abrupt fulminant phase with shock/toxemia. Hallmark imaging = widened mediastinum (hemorrhagic mediastinitis). Not person-to-person contagious.

nodes.label: Index-Case Recognition (Biological Attack)
nodes.rationale: A bioterror release is silent and declares itself as a cluster of unusually severe/unusual disease. The clinician who recognizes the index case triggers the population-saving public-health response (epidemiology, PEP, countermeasures).

nodes.label: Anthrax Treatment vs Post-Exposure Prophylaxis
nodes.rationale: Active disease: aggressive IV combination antibiotics (fluoroquinolone e.g. ciprofloxacin + protein-synthesis inhibitor), antitoxin (raxibacumab/obiltoxaximab), intensive support, meningitis coverage. PEP for the well exposed: oral cipro/doxycycline 60 days + vaccine to outlast spore germination.

nodes.label: Sentinel / Public-Health Integration
nodes.rationale: The medic is a sentinel and node in a public-health system: notify public health/command immediately (reporting triggers the response), preserve specimens, support epidemiology and mass PEP/countermeasure delivery. Containing bioterror is a public-health operation.

nodes.label: Scenario 20 — Wildfire Entrapment: Burns & Inhalation Injury (Operation Trapped Flame)
nodes.rationale: Casualty overrun by fire escaping an enclosed vehicle: deep face/neck/arm burns + inhalation injury + developing stridor + CO/cyanide. RMH Burns p.54-55 / Inhalation Injury / Carbon Monoxide.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 20 — wildfire entrapment: burns & inhalation injury (operation trapped flame)

nodes.label: Inhalation Injury / Early Airway Control
nodes.rationale: In fire entrapment the airway, not the burn, is the emergency: hot gases inflame and progressively swell the upper airway, which doesn't reopen. Signs (singed nares, hoarseness, soot, stridor, enclosed-space fire) trigger early definitive airway control before edema makes it impossible.

nodes.label: Carbon Monoxide Poisoning
nodes.rationale: CO binds hemoglobin and makes the pulse-ox read falsely normal (96% while blood carries poison). High-flow 100% oxygen is the antidote and shortens CO's half-life; disregard a reassuring SpO2.

nodes.label: Cyanide Toxicity / Hydroxocobalamin
nodes.rationale: Cyanide from burning synthetics poisons cellular energy production causing severe metabolic acidosis/collapse; specific antidote hydroxocobalamin at higher care. Consider in enclosed/vehicle fires regardless of SpO2.

nodes.label: Burn Fluid Resuscitation (Rule of Nines / Parkland)
nodes.rationale: Estimate TBSA by rule of nines; Parkland-type fluid resuscitation titrated to urine output (too little = burn shock/renal injury, too much = worse edema). Runs in parallel with — not ahead of — airway and CO.
nodes.norm_label: burn fluid resuscitation (rule of nines / parkland)

nodes.label: Circumferential Burns / Escharotomy
nodes.rationale: Deep circumferential neck/chest burns form rigid eschar that compresses the airway or restricts chest expansion, mechanically choking ventilation; may need escharotomy (surgical eschar release) at definitive care. Flag the need for the receiving facility.

nodes.label: NORTHCOM Theater (CBRN / Homeland Defense / DSCA)
nodes.rationale: NORTHCOM scenarios center on CBRN, mass-casualty, disaster, and homeland-defense medicine delivered as Defense Support of Civil Authorities (DSCA) within a civilian-led incident command — the medic acts as a sentinel/first link who recognizes, isolates/decontaminates, treats, reports, and integrates into the public-health/law-enforcement response.

nodes.label: Scenario 21 — Smallpox: Recognition, Isolation & Ring Vaccination (OPERATION POX SHADOW)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 21 — smallpox: recognition, isolation & ring vaccination (operation pox shadow)

nodes.label: Smallpox (Variola)
nodes.rationale: Eradicated in 1980, so any genuine case implies deliberate release (bioterrorism); recognized by a synchronous (same-stage), centrifugal (face/extremities/palms/soles), deep pustular rash after a toxic febrile prodrome — distinguished from chickenpox (asynchronous, superficial, centripetal/trunk, spares palms/soles).

nodes.label: Ring Vaccination
nodes.rationale: Containment strategy: vaccinate the ring of contacts and contacts-of-contacts around each case; works because post-exposure vaccination within days can prevent/attenuate disease — the strategy that originally eradicated smallpox.

nodes.label: Public-Health Emergency Response (Contact Tracing / Containment)
nodes.rationale: Recurring framework across NORTHCOM bioterror scenarios: the medic is a sentinel/tripwire who recognizes, isolates, reports emergently, preserves specimens under biosafety, and plugs into a civilian-led containment operation (case finding, contact tracing, mass prophylaxis/vaccination).

nodes.label: Scenario 22 — Pneumonic Plague: Aerosolized Yersinia & Contagious Spread (OPERATION DARK BUBO)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 22 — pneumonic plague: aerosolized yersinia & contagious spread (operation dark bubo)

nodes.label: Pneumonic Plague (Yersinia pestis)
nodes.rationale: Weaponizable, contagious lung form (vs. flea-borne bubonic); a cluster of fulminant pneumonia + hemoptysis with short uniform incubation (2-4 days) after shared exposure suggests aerosol release. Category A agent; rapidly fatal so treat early on suspicion.

nodes.label: Scenario 23 — Botulism: Descending Paralysis & Antitoxin Timing (OPERATION SILENT PALSY)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 23 — botulism: descending paralysis & antitoxin timing (operation silent palsy)

nodes.label: Botulism (Botulinum Toxin)
nodes.rationale: Most potent biological toxin; blocks acetylcholine release at the neuromuscular junction (mirror image of nerve agents). Causes symmetric descending flaccid paralysis from cranial nerves (the 4 Ds), clear mentation, no fever — distinguished from ascending Guillain-Barre.

nodes.label: Botulinum Antitoxin
nodes.rationale: Neutralizes only circulating (unbound) toxin and halts progression — it does NOT reverse existing paralysis. Give early (within ~24-48h) on clinical suspicion without awaiting labs; secured via the CDC Botulism Service 24/7.

nodes.label: Airway / Ventilatory Support
nodes.rationale: Mainstay of botulism care: descending paralysis reaches respiratory muscles, so vigilant airway monitoring and mechanical ventilation (weeks-months) keep the patient alive while the toxin effect wears off — a mass release threatens to overwhelm ventilator capacity.

nodes.label: Scenario 24 — Ricin Exposure: Toxin Poisoning by Route (OPERATION BITTER SEED)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 24 — ricin exposure: toxin poisoning by route (operation bitter seed)

nodes.label: Ricin (Castor-Bean Toxin)
nodes.rationale: Derived from castor beans; shuts down cellular protein synthesis by inactivating ribosomes — NO antidote. Route shapes the disease: inhalation (most lethal, delayed respiratory/pulmonary edema), ingestion (severe GI), injection (systemic organ failure). Not contagious — hazard is the material, so PPE/decon not patient isolation.

nodes.label: Supportive / Critical Care (No-Antidote Toolkit)
nodes.rationale: Recurring mainstay for high-consequence agents with no/limited specific therapy: support the failing organs (oxygenation, ventilation, fluids, blood products) while the agent's damage runs its course.

nodes.label: Law-Enforcement / FBI Bioterror Investigation Integration
nodes.rationale: A deliberate-release incident is simultaneously a medical event, a crime scene, and a public-health concern — the medic preserves material/specimens as evidence and integrates with the multi-agency response.

nodes.label: Delayed-Onset Inhalational Injury ('Calm Before the Storm')
nodes.rationale: Inhaled agents (ricin, chlorine, blast lung) can deteriorate hours after exposure; absence of early severe symptoms is not reassurance — observe and evacuate exposed casualties, don't clear on scene.
nodes.norm_label: delayed-onset inhalational injury ('calm before the storm')

nodes.label: Scenario 25 — Viral Hemorrhagic Fever: High-Consequence Isolation & PPE (OPERATION CRIMSON VEIL)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 25 — viral hemorrhagic fever: high-consequence isolation & ppe (operation crimson veil)

nodes.label: Viral Hemorrhagic Fever (e.g., Ebola-type)
nodes.rationale: Fever + GI symptoms then a bleeding diathesis (mucosal bleeding, bruising, GI hemorrhage) and shock; body fluids are intensely contagious and lethal, so rigorous isolation and disciplined PPE (especially supervised doffing) are the dominant priority — a single breach can be fatal.

nodes.label: Scenario 26 — Tularemia: Aerosolized Francisella & Atypical Pneumonia Cluster (OPERATION GRAY HARVEST)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 26 — tularemia: aerosolized francisella & atypical pneumonia cluster (operation gray harvest)

nodes.label: Scenario 27 — Waterborne/Foodborne Outbreak: Mass GI Illness & Sanitation Collapse (OPERATION TAINTED SPRING)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 27 — waterborne/foodborne outbreak: mass gi illness & sanitation collapse (operation tainted spring)

nodes.label: Scenario 28 — Pandemic Mass Triage: Crisis Standards of Care & Scarce Resources (OPERATION QUIET SURGE)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 28 — pandemic mass triage: crisis standards of care & scarce resources (operation quiet surge)

nodes.label: Crisis Standards of Care (Scarce-Resource Allocation)
nodes.rationale: Framework for allocating scarce resources during pandemic/MASCAL surge; shifts from individual best-care to population-benefit, with shared, principled decisions rather than one provider's burden.

nodes.label: Scenario 29 — Emerging Pathogen: Novel Outbreak & Diagnostic Uncertainty (OPERATION UNKNOWN VECTOR)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 29 — emerging pathogen: novel outbreak & diagnostic uncertainty (operation unknown vector)

nodes.label: Scenario 30 — Biological MASCAL: Mass Prophylaxis & Stockpile (POD) Operations (OPERATION SHIELD WALL)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 30 — biological mascal: mass prophylaxis & stockpile (pod) operations (operation shield wall)

nodes.label: Point of Dispensing (POD) / Mass Prophylaxis Operations
nodes.norm_label: point of dispensing (pod) / mass prophylaxis operations

nodes.label: Scenario 31 — Improvised Nuclear Device: Detonation Response & 'Get Inside' (OPERATION IRON SUNRISE)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 31 — improvised nuclear device: detonation response & 'get inside' (operation iron sunrise)

nodes.label: 'Get Inside, Stay Inside, Stay Tuned' (Fallout Shelter Guidance)
nodes.norm_label: 'get inside, stay inside, stay tuned' (fallout shelter guidance)

nodes.label: Scenario 32 — Acute Radiation Syndrome: Dose Estimation & Sub-Syndromes (OPERATION PALE HORIZON)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 32 — acute radiation syndrome: dose estimation & sub-syndromes (operation pale horizon)

nodes.label: Scenario 33 — Nuclear Reactor Incident: Radioiodine & Potassium Iodide (OPERATION BROKEN ATOM)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 33 — nuclear reactor incident: radioiodine & potassium iodide (operation broken atom)

nodes.label: Scenario 34 — Combined Injury: Radiation Plus Trauma & the Surgical Window (OPERATION TWICE STRUCK)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 34 — combined injury: radiation plus trauma & the surgical window (operation twice struck)

nodes.label: Combined Injury (Radiation + Trauma)
nodes.rationale: Radiation impairs healing and immunity, so definitive surgery must occur within an early window before hematopoietic suppression sets in; combined injury worsens prognosis synergistically.

nodes.label: Scenario 35 — Decontamination Operations: Zones, Throughput & Cross-Contamination (OPERATION CLEAN SWEEP)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 35 — decontamination operations: zones, throughput & cross-contamination (operation clean sweep)

nodes.label: Scenario 36 — Internal Contamination: Decorporation Therapy & Radionuclide Matching (OPERATION INNER TIDE)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 36 — internal contamination: decorporation therapy & radionuclide matching (operation inner tide)

nodes.label: Decorporation Therapy (Radionuclide-Matched: Prussian Blue / DTPA)
nodes.rationale: Internal contamination treatment is radionuclide-specific — the chelating/binding agent must be matched to the isotope (e.g., Prussian blue for cesium/thallium, DTPA for transuranics).

nodes.label: Scenario 37 — Cutaneous Radiation Injury: Localized Radiation Burns (OPERATION SCORCHED SKIN)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 37 — cutaneous radiation injury: localized radiation burns (operation scorched skin)

nodes.label: Scenario 38 — Targeted Internal Poisoning: Covert Radionuclide & Diagnostic Puzzle (OPERATION HIDDEN ISOTOPE)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 38 — targeted internal poisoning: covert radionuclide & diagnostic puzzle (operation hidden isotope)

nodes.label: Scenario 39 — Infrastructure Collapse: Medicine Without Power, Comms, or Resupply (OPERATION DARK GRID)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 39 — infrastructure collapse: medicine without power, comms, or resupply (operation dark grid)

nodes.label: Scenario 40 — CBRN with Degraded Protection: Limited PPE & Risk Decisions (OPERATION BROKEN SEAL)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 40 — cbrn with degraded protection: limited ppe & risk decisions (operation broken seal)

nodes.label: Degraded-PPE Risk-Benefit Decision (Rescuer Protection vs. Casualty Care)
nodes.rationale: In a prolonged CBRN event 'protect yourself first' becomes a deliberate risk calculation — weigh threat, actual protection, casualty salvageability/benefit, and exposure; neither reckless (become a casualty) nor paralyzed (abandon salvageable casualties).
nodes.norm_label: degraded-ppe risk-benefit decision (rescuer protection vs. casualty care)

nodes.label: Scenario 41 — Border/Migrant Medical: Austere Care for a Vulnerable Population (OPERATION OPEN BORDER)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 41 — border/migrant medical: austere care for a vulnerable population (operation open border)

nodes.label: Austere Humanitarian Care for a Vulnerable Migrant Population
nodes.rationale: Layered mix: environmental injury (dehydration/heat), decompensated untreated chronic disease, infectious disease (crowding/sanitation), trauma, and vulnerable groups (children, pregnant women) — treat the acute environmental threat first, manage infectious disease at population level, and care for all with dignity regardless of legal status within a complex DSCA/interagency legal framework.
nodes.norm_label: austere humanitarian care for a vulnerable migrant population

nodes.label: DSCA / Civilian-Led Incident Command Integration
nodes.rationale: Defense Support of Civil Authorities — the military medic supports a civilian-led response (CDC, public health, CBP, law enforcement) and provides care based on medical need/ethics while legal/policy dimensions are handled by appropriate authorities.

nodes.label: Category A Bioterrorism Agents
nodes.rationale: High-priority, high-consequence bioterror agents (smallpox, plague, tularemia, botulism, VHF, anthrax) whose recognition by a sentinel medic triggers a coordinated containment/public-health response.

nodes.label: Nerve Agents (Acetylcholine Excess — mirror of botulism)
nodes.rationale: Botulism (acetylcholine blockade) is described as the mirror image of nerve agents, which cause acetylcholine excess (cholinergic crisis).
nodes.norm_label: nerve agents (acetylcholine excess — mirror of botulism)

nodes.label: Care Based on Need & Human Dignity (impartial medical ethics)
nodes.rationale: The medic's ethical duty is to provide medical care based on need and human dignity regardless of legal/political status — recurring DSCA principle applied to migrant population and civil-unrest casualties: treat the person in front of you.
nodes.norm_label: care based on need & human dignity (impartial medical ethics)

nodes.label: Acute Environmental Injury — Dehydration / Heat (treat first)
nodes.norm_label: acute environmental injury — dehydration / heat (treat first)

nodes.label: NORTHCOM Scenario 42 — Civil Unrest: Crowd-Control Agents & Kinetic-Impact Injuries (Operation Still Waters)
nodes.norm_label: northcom scenario 42 — civil unrest: crowd-control agents & kinetic-impact injuries (operation still waters)

nodes.label: Kinetic-Impact-Projectile ('Rubber Bullet') Trauma — Treat as Genuine Trauma
nodes.rationale: 'Less lethal' does not mean harmless — kinetic projectiles can cause fractures, organ/intracranial/penetrating injury and death; run a full trauma survey, don't be lulled by the label.
nodes.norm_label: kinetic-impact-projectile ('rubber bullet') trauma — treat as genuine trauma

nodes.label: Delayed Pulmonary Effects from Heavy Agent Exposure
nodes.norm_label: delayed pulmonary effects from heavy agent exposure

nodes.label: Neutral, Dignified Care in a Politically Charged Environment
nodes.rationale: Medical neutrality — treat all casualties as patients based on need regardless of politics; both an ethical principle and practical (preserves trust and ability to function), same principle as the border/humanitarian scenario.
nodes.norm_label: neutral, dignified care in a politically charged environment

nodes.label: NORTHCOM Scenario 43 — Protective Medicine / VIP Dignitary Support (Operation Close Guard)
nodes.norm_label: northcom scenario 43 — protective medicine / vip dignitary support (operation close guard)

nodes.label: Anticipation & Readiness — Core Skill of Protective Medicine
nodes.rationale: Protective medicine is won in the planning before the event — advance medical planning for the principal's known needs AND a sudden attack, pre-positioning, pre-coordination, and rehearsal; when an attack occurs there's no time to figure out logistics.
nodes.norm_label: anticipation & readiness — core skill of protective medicine

nodes.label: Mission-Priority Tension — Principal vs. Bystanders
nodes.rationale: Mission priorities legitimately shape medical priorities: the protective medic's primary duty is the principal while bystanders are covered by other responders/local EMS — a real ethical/operational tension the medic must understand in advance.

nodes.label: Pre-Coordination with Local EMS / Hospitals / Routes
nodes.norm_label: pre-coordination with local ems / hospitals / routes

nodes.label: NORTHCOM Scenario 44 — Telemedicine Reach-Back (Operation Long Wire)
nodes.norm_label: northcom scenario 44 — telemedicine reach-back (operation long wire)

nodes.label: Clear Clinical Communication — Make-or-Break Reach-Back Skill
nodes.rationale: The remote physician can only help based on the picture the medic paints — structured handoff, accurate exam, precise updates — the same picture-painting underlying any good handoff, now load-bearing as the physician's only window into the patient.
nodes.norm_label: clear clinical communication — make-or-break reach-back skill

nodes.label: Plan for Intermittent Connectivity / Retain Autonomous Capability
nodes.norm_label: plan for intermittent connectivity / retain autonomous capability

nodes.label: Prolonged Casualty Care (hours-to-days to definitive care)
nodes.norm_label: prolonged casualty care (hours-to-days to definitive care)

nodes.label: NORTHCOM Scenario 45 — EMS/ICS Integration: Military-Civilian Seam (Operation Common Cause)
nodes.norm_label: northcom scenario 45 — ems/ics integration: military-civilian seam (operation common cause)

nodes.label: DSCA Doctrine — Civilian-Led, Military-Supporting (Posse Comitatus)
nodes.rationale: Domestic disaster response is civilian-led; the military supports civil authorities within ICS and the legal framework (Posse Comitatus/DSCA authorities). Command-relationship clarity prevents conflicting orders and legal/operational problems.

nodes.label: Plain-Language MIST Handoffs Across the Seam
nodes.norm_label: plain-language mist handoffs across the seam

nodes.label: Integration as the Ultimate DSCA Force-Multiplier
nodes.rationale: Clean integration lets military capability fill the civilian system's gaps additively; the same clinical skill integrated well helps far more people — integration skill matters as much as clinical skill. This is the synthesis of the DSCA thread running through the whole library.
nodes.norm_label: integration as the ultimate dsca force-multiplier

nodes.label: NORTHCOM Scenario 46 — Wide-Area Disaster: Medical Logistics & Sustained Operations (Operation Long Relief)
nodes.norm_label: northcom scenario 46 — wide-area disaster: medical logistics & sustained operations (operation long relief)

nodes.label: Medical Logistics — Backbone of Sustained Disaster Operations
nodes.rationale: At scale and over time, getting the right resources to the right places reliably is the central decisive challenge — a field full of skilled medics accomplishes little without supplies; well-run logistics lets modest clinical capability help enormous numbers.
nodes.norm_label: medical logistics — backbone of sustained disaster operations

nodes.label: Prevention / Public Health (WASH, surveillance) — Prevents Secondary Crises
nodes.norm_label: prevention / public health (wash, surveillance) — prevents secondary crises

nodes.label: NORTHCOM Scenario 47 — Domestic Terror Bombing: Blast Injury Mass Casualty (Operation Shattered Square)
nodes.norm_label: northcom scenario 47 — domestic terror bombing: blast injury mass casualty (operation shattered square)

nodes.label: Blast Lung — Deadly, Often-Occult Primary Overpressure Injury
nodes.rationale: Blast lung is a leading cause of death in blast survivors and can be invisible initially, deteriorating over minutes-to-hours; enclosed-space blasts amplify it — actively seek it, don't clear stable-looking blast victims.
nodes.norm_label: blast lung — deadly, often-occult primary overpressure injury

nodes.label: Secondary-Device Threat — Care Under Threat / Scene Safety
nodes.rationale: Bombings may have a second device timed to kill responders — the domestic-terrorism parallel to battlefield care under fire; a responder killed adds to the casualties, so scene safety and self-protection are paramount.
nodes.norm_label: secondary-device threat — care under threat / scene safety

nodes.label: NORTHCOM Scenario 49 — Behavioral Health & Responder Resilience (Operation Steady Hand)
nodes.norm_label: northcom scenario 49 — behavioral health & responder resilience (operation steady hand)

nodes.label: Psychological First Aid (PFA) — Not Formal Therapy
nodes.norm_label: psychological first aid (pfa) — not formal therapy

nodes.label: Don't Overpathologize Normal Stress / Reduce Stigma
nodes.rationale: Most reactions to abnormal traumatic events are normal and self-resolving; labeling them as disorders undermines resilience and creates stigma. Normalize and support common reactions while escalating the severe/persistent/impairing minority to formal care; seeking help is strength, not weakness.
nodes.norm_label: don't overpathologize normal stress / reduce stigma

nodes.label: NORTHCOM Scenario 50 — Integrated Multi-Threat Homeland Capstone
nodes.rationale: The capstone is fundamentally a problem of INTEGRATION/synthesis — sequencing care-under-threat, CBRN, MASCAL triage, decon, crisis standards, population protection, and civilian integration all at once. Integration, not isolated skill, is the capstone competency.
nodes.norm_label: northcom scenario 50 — integrated multi-threat homeland capstone

nodes.label: Self/Team Protection — Non-Negotiable Foundation
nodes.rationale: A protected, sustained responder helps casualties while a careless one becomes one; the homeland catastrophe is exactly where the temptation to rush in is greatest and the cost of becoming a casualty is highest — the overwhelmed system cannot afford to lose responders.

nodes.label: 75th Ranger Regiment — Zero Prehospital Preventable Combat Death Record
nodes.rationale: A 2024 Military Medicine review documented 813 battle-injury casualties over 20 years with a zero rate of prehospital preventable combat death — no fatalities from isolated extremity hemorrhage, tension pneumothorax, or airway obstruction.
nodes.norm_label: 75th ranger regiment — zero prehospital preventable combat death record

nodes.label: Regiment's Five Casualty-Response Principles
nodes.rationale: (1) TCCC mastery for ALL Rangers; (2) far-forward blood via ROLO; (3) command ownership of the casualty-response system; (4) continuous documentation & performance improvement; (5) master the basics before advanced skills.

nodes.label: CTLS Task List (331-SOM-XXXX) + 2025 Ranger Medic Handbook Protocols
nodes.norm_label: ctls task list (331-som-xxxx) + 2025 ranger medic handbook protocols

nodes.label: Ranger Medic Scenario 1 — IED Strike: TCCC & Massive Hemorrhage Control (Operation Resolute Strike)
nodes.norm_label: ranger medic scenario 1 — ied strike: tccc & massive hemorrhage control (operation resolute strike)

nodes.label: Care Under Fire — Fire Superiority is the Best Medicine
nodes.rationale: In care under fire the immediate priority is the threat, not the wound: return fire and win the firefight, direct casualty self-aid, then move when tactically feasible. The only medical intervention in this phase is a limb tourniquet — you can't run a code in a kill zone.
nodes.norm_label: care under fire — fire superiority is the best medicine

nodes.label: Junctional Hemorrhage Control (CAT, 2nd TQ, junctional TQ, packing, clamping)
nodes.norm_label: junctional hemorrhage control (cat, 2nd tq, junctional tq, packing, clamping)

nodes.label: Permissive Hypotension (no-TBI target: palpable radial pulse / SBP 80-90)
nodes.rationale: Keep just enough pressure to perfuse brain/heart while letting the clot hold — restoring normal pressure with aggressive fluids blows the clot off; the exception is TBI, which needs higher perfusion pressure. Give blood, not crystalloid.
nodes.norm_label: permissive hypotension (no-tbi target: palpable radial pulse / sbp 80-90)

nodes.label: ROLO Whole Blood Transfusion (Ranger O Low-Titer)
nodes.norm_label: rolo whole blood transfusion (ranger o low-titer)

nodes.label: TXA 2 g IV/IO within 3 Hours — Never Delay Blood for TXA
nodes.rationale: TXA is the clot protector, blood is the clot material — material first then lock it in place; RMH p.29 explicit: do not delay blood to give TXA. Must be within 3 hours of injury or it can harm.
nodes.norm_label: txa 2 g iv/io within 3 hours — never delay blood for txa

nodes.norm_label: scenario 11: severe hypothermia — afterdrop & gentle rewarming

nodes.norm_label: scenario 12: community-acquired pneumonia vs altitude illness

nodes.norm_label: scenario 16: behavioral health crisis — suicidal ideation
